products.title: Green Tiger Lotus
products.body_html:
Nymphaea Zenkeri 'Green' also is known as the Green Tiger Lotus. It's a flowering plant of the family Nymphaeaceae that grows in various parts of East Africa. This species is known for its brilliant reddish-purple-tinted heart-shaped leaves. This is the most popular tropical waterlily in the aquarium industry.
The Tiger Lotus is undemanding. It should be planted in a rich-based nutrient substrate and good lighting. When grow floating with strong or natural sunlight, it can bloom with white, fragrant flowers that are open from evening to late morning. 
  • Sold as a Tiger Lotus Plant or Tiger Lotus Bulb
  • We do not guarantee the bulb to grow even though the bulb has very high sprout rates.
  • Simply wash them in clean water and then drop them in the aquarium or pond. Under optimal aquarium conditions, you will notice new growth within 10-15 days.
  • Pictures are not the actual plants you will receive, but a sample representation.
CO2: Not Required
Growth Rates: Fast
Difficulty Level: Beginner
Nutrient Substrate: Not Required
Lighting Requirements: Low-High

Green Tiger Lotus - Aquarium Plants Factory

products.title: Bolbitis Heudelotii Difformis
products.body_html:
Bolbitis heudelotii 'Difformis'

A compact, ruffled twist on the African Water Fern

Bolbitis heudelotii 'Difformis' is a rare and compact variant of the classic African Water Fern, cherished by aquascapers for its distinctive, finely dissected fronds and slower growth habit. With lush green, feathered foliage and a delicate, curly structure, it brings a graceful, forest-like ambiance to any aquarium setting—especially in shaded or low-flow areas.

This species is epiphytic by nature and thrives when attached to hardscape such as driftwood or porous rock. Its small, wavy-edged leaves and moderate pace make it ideal for nano tanks, low-tech aquascapes, or aquariums with shrimp and delicate fish species.

🌿 Key Features

  • Scientific Name: Bolbitis heudelotii 'Difformis'

  • Common Name: Mini African Water Fern or Dwarf Bolbitis

  • Growth Form: Rhizomatous epiphyte

  • Leaf Size: Smaller and more dissected than standard Bolbitis heudelotii

  • Placement: Midground to background; best attached to rock or wood

  • Growth Rate: Slow to moderate

💡 Optimal Growing Conditions

  • Lighting: Low to medium light preferred; high light may encourage algae on leaves

  • CO₂: Not required, but enhances growth and coloration

  • Water Parameters:

    • pH: 5.5 – 7.5

    • Temperature: 68 – 79°F (20 – 26°C)

    • Water Hardness: Soft to moderately hard

  • Flow: Gentle to moderate; avoid high turbulence

🌱 Aquascaping Benefits

  • Creates a lush, natural look with minimal maintenance

  • Ideal for shady or low-tech tanks

  • Provides excellent shelter for shrimp and fry

  • Pairs beautifully with mosses, Anubias, and Bucephalandra

✂️ Propagation & Growth

  • Nutrient Substrate: Not required (rhizome should not be buried)

  • Can Be Grown Emersed: Yes, under high humidity

  • Propagation occurs by dividing the rhizome. Simply cut between leaf clusters and reattach to hardscape for new growth to begin.

📦 What You’ll Receive

Available in three formats:

  • Bare-Root Plants: 4+ healthy fronds with rhizome, loose for flexible placement

  • Bunched Plants: 4+ fronds tied with foam and weight for easy setup

  • Potted Plants: 4+ fronds rooted in rockwool and net pot for secure anchoring

⚠️ State Restrictions Notice ♻️ Be a Responsible Aquatic Plants Owner


Please check your state regulations before purchasing. Bolbitis species are generally unrestricted, but always verify for local compliance.

📢 Disclaimer

As a slow-growing fern, Bolbitis heudelotii 'Difformis' may take time to adjust and establish in new environments. Avoid burying the rhizome in substrate, as it may rot. Attach with thread or glue to wood or rock for best results.

featured_image.alt: Bolbitis heudelotii difformis is a variation of the Bolbitis heudelotii species that is characterized by its unique frond shapes and compact growth habit. It is a popular choice for aquascaping due to its attractive appearance and ease of care.
Discover the elegant Bolbitis heudelotii 'Difformis', a rare dwarf variant of the African Water Fern. Ideal for aquascaping with low light and gentle flow setups.
Potted Bolbitis heudelotii 'Difformis' showing lush 4+ leaf growth in a net pot with rockwool.

products.title: Echinodorus Aflame Vitro Culture (Purple Knight Sword)
products.body_html:
🛡️ Echinodorus Aflame Vitro Culture – Purple Knight Sword in 4-Inch Bag

Echinodorus Aflame, commonly known as the Purple Knight Sword, is a captivating compact sword plant admired for its deep burgundy to dark purple foliage and striking upright rosette form. Cultivated in a sterile Vitro Culture environment, this plant arrives in a sealed 4-inch bag, containing approximately 3–4 small, healthy plantlets—ready to bring a bold, regal presence to your aquascape.

Perfect as a midground or background accent, the Purple Knight Sword thrives under high light and enriched substrate, with CO₂ supplementation helping it reach its full crimson potential. Its slow to moderate growth makes it manageable, while its color and structure create a commanding focal point.

✨ Key Features:

  • Scientific Name: Echinodorus 'Aflame'

  • Common Name: Purple Knight Sword

  • Growth Form: Upright, compact rosette with sword-like leaves

  • Coloration: Deep red to purple-black under optimal light

  • Light Demand: Medium to high

  • CO₂ Requirement: Recommended for best color development

  • Growth Rate: Medium

  • Placement: Midground to background

🌿 Optimal Growing Conditions:

  • Temperature: 72–82°F (22–28°C)

  • pH Range: 6.0–7.5

  • Water Hardness: Soft to moderately hard

  • Lighting: Strong lighting enhances deep red coloration

  • Substrate: Nutrient-rich substrate is required for optimal performance

🌊 Vitro Culture Advantages:

  • Pest-Free & Clean: 100% free from snails, algae, and pesticides

  • High Yield: Each 4-inch bag provides approximately 3–4 plantlets

  • Safe for Shrimp & Fry: Excellent for sensitive or display tanks

  • Compact & Adaptable: Easy to plant and acclimate into new setups

🔁 Propagation & Growth:

  • Propagation: Produces adventitious plantlets on submerged flower stalks

  • Nutrient Substrate: Required

  • Can Be Grown Emersed: Yes – thrives in humid paludariums and terrariums

📦 What You’ll Receive:

  • You will receive one sealed 4-inch Vitro Culture bag containing approximately 3–4 young Echinodorus Aflame plantlets

⚠️ Disclaimer:

Plant coloration may vary based on lighting, nutrients, and CO₂ levels. Initial growth may appear greenish and gradually develop intense red or purple hues. Avoid burying the rosette crown to prevent rot.

products.title: Pogostemon Deccanensis Vitro Culture (Pogostemon Erectus)
products.body_html:
🌿 Pogostemon deccanensis Vitro Culture® – Indian Star Plant with Lush Needle-Like Foliage

Enhance your aquarium with the striking beauty of Pogostemon deccanensis, also known as the Indian Star plant. Formerly mislabeled in the hobby as Pogostemon erectus, this stunning species has since been correctly identified as Pogostemon deccanensis. Now cultivated using our Vitro Culture® method, it arrives completely pest-free, algae-free, and pesticide-free, ensuring a clean and healthy start for any aquascape.

This stem plant is known for its fine, needle-like foliage that resembles conifer branches, creating compact vertical clusters of vibrant green. Its lush, feathery texture adds motion and contrast when planted among broader-leaved or red-hued plants—making it a dynamic choice for both midground and background areas of the aquarium.

🌟 Key Features:

  • Scientific Name: Pogostemon deccanensis

  • Synonym: Pogostemon erectus (obsolete)

  • Common Name: Indian Star Plant

  • Leaf Appearance: Fine, needle-like green foliage, dense and bushy

  • Growth Form: Upright vertical stems with compact foliage clusters

  • Placement: Midground to background

  • Growth Rate: Moderate

  • Difficulty Level: Easy to Medium

🌱 Growth & Propagation:

  • Lighting Needs: Moderate to high for compact and healthy growth

  • CO₂ & Fertilizers: CO₂ beneficial but not strictly required; regular fertilization promotes optimal color and density

  • Growth Method: Submerged only

  • Nutrient Substrate: Recommended

  • Can Be Grown Emersed: No

🖼️ Aquascaping Benefits:

  • Creates soft, textured clusters resembling miniature conifers

  • Adds vibrant light green contrast to darker or red plant species

  • Great for midground structure or soft background layering

📦 What You’ll Receive:

One 4-inch sealed Vitro Culture® bag containing multiple healthy Pogostemon deccanensis plantlets—ready to rinse, acclimate, and plant in your aquarium.

⚠️ Disclaimer:

Newly introduced Pogostemon deccanensis may take time to adjust to submerged conditions. Yellowing or melt during early stages is common and will improve with stable conditions and light.

🚫 State Restrictions Notice ♻️ Be a Responsible Aquatic Plants Owner

Never release Pogostemon deccanensis—or any aquarium plant—into natural waterways. Doing so can disrupt local ecosystems. Dispose of unwanted portions responsibly to help preserve aquatic biodiversity.

products.title: Cryptocoryne Parva Mini Vitro Culture
products.body_html:
Because of its tiny size, Cryptocoryne Parva is considered the smallest Cryptocoryne species. Cryptocoryne Parva Mini, which is even smaller than the renowned Cryptocoryne Parva, is currently the tiniest Cryptocoryne.

Minimalist in size, Cryptocoryne Parva Mini is a nano-sized plant. As a carpet foreground plant, it is green in color and grows very compactly and low to the ground. Rumors have it that Cryptocoryne Parva Mini originated in Indonesia, however, we have not been able to confirm this. Our knowledge of its origin is limited, but we suspect it is a 'lab-made' product, which means it is derived from tissue culture and may have been altered by humans to achieve its amazing size.

Regardless, this is a good plant for creating a carpet appearance in the aquarium.
Due to the plant's tiny size, freshwater shrimp, such as Amano Shrimp and Red Cherry Shrimp, are great for cleaning and eating algae off it.

  • Smallest Cryptocoryne species in the market
  • Sold as a Tissue Culture Plants with vitamin liquid/gel
  • Packaging May Vary! To be sent in either in vitro bag or cup
  • Bag / Cup size: Approx. 2.5 x 2.5 inches
  • Sustainable growth aquarium plants species
  • Great value! Nice specimen!
  • Tissue Culture Technology | 100% Free from Snail, Algae, Pathogen, and Bacteria plus Extended Shelf Life on all in Vitro Plants

Disclaimer: Pictures are not the actual plants you will receive, but a sample representation. Unless specific, we don't guarantee aquatic plants will be free of pests, and pesticides, grow emerged or submerge.

products.title: Cryptocoryne Spiralis Tiger Vitro Culture
products.body_html:
Cryptocoryne spiralis 'Tiger' Vitro Culture® – Bold Tiger-Striped Foliage for Aquascaping Impact

Cryptocoryne spiralis 'Tiger' is a dramatic and eye-catching rosette plant with long, spiraled leaves marked by bold tiger-like striping. Native to India, it adds a wild, natural look to midground or background areas of aquascapes. With colors ranging from bronze to reddish-brown and a pattern that mimics tiger stripes, this unique variety of Cryptocoryne brings depth and motion to planted tanks.

Delivered in our Vitro Culture® bag format, this plant is grown using sterile tissue culture methods and packaged in nutrient gel to ensure a clean, pest-free start—perfect for shrimp tanks, nano tanks, and sensitive environments.

🌿 Key Features:

  • Scientific Name: Cryptocoryne spiralis 'Tiger'

  • Common Name: Crypt Tiger Spiralis

  • Leaf Shape: Long, narrow, spiraled with dark striping

  • Coloration: Bronze to reddish-brown with tiger-like markings

  • Light Demand: Low to medium

  • CO₂ Requirement: Optional, but enhances coloration

  • Growth Rate: Slow to moderate

  • Placement: Midground to background

🧫 Vitro Culture® Advantages:

  • Grown in sterile lab conditions

  • Free from snails, algae, and harmful pathogens

  • Sealed in a clear, nutrient-rich bag for freshness

  • No pesticides—completely shrimp-safe

  • Easy to rinse and plant upon arrival

🌱 Aquascaping Benefits:

  • Adds visual texture and contrast to planted tanks

  • Excellent for jungle, biotope, and nature-style layouts

  • Pairs beautifully with smaller Crypts, Anubias, or Bucephalandra

🌿 Propagation & Growth:

  • Propagation: Via runners or side shoots

  • Nutrient Substrate: ✅ Required

  • Can Be Grown Emersed: ✅ Yes (ideal for terrariums, paludariums, and emersed setups)

📦 What You’ll Receive:

  • 1 sealed Vitro Culture® bag containing clean, healthy Cryptocoryne spiralis 'Tiger' plantlets. Yield approx. 3-4 clumps.

⚠️ Disclaimer:

Cryptocoryne species often experience “melting” when adjusting to new conditions. This is normal. Ensure stable lighting and parameters, and the plant will regrow once established.

products.title: Red Root Floater
products.body_html:
Red Root Floater (Phyllanthus fluitans) are a striking and vibrant addition to any freshwater aquarium or pond. Known for their beautiful red roots and green leaves that turn a stunning reddish hue under optimal conditions, these floating plants add a unique and eye-catching element to aquatic landscapes.

Key Features:

  • Vibrant Coloration: Features green leaves that develop a reddish tint and striking red roots, enhancing the visual appeal of any water feature.
  • Floating Plant: As a floating plant, Red Root Floaters do not need to be planted in substrate, making them easy to add to any setup.

Optimal Conditions:

  • Lighting: Thrives under moderate to high lighting conditions, which promotes the red coloration of the leaves and roots.
  • Water Parameters: Prefers slightly acidic to neutral pH levels (6.0 to 7.5) and temperatures between 70°F to 82°F (21°C to 28°C).

Aquarium and Pond Benefits:

  • Shade and Shelter: Provides shade and shelter for fish and other aquatic inhabitants, helping to reduce stress and create a more natural environment.
  • Nutrient Absorption: Helps absorb excess nutrients from the water, reducing algae growth and improving water quality.
  • Aesthetic Appeal: Adds a unique and colorful element to the water surface, enhancing the overall aesthetic of aquariums and ponds.

Care and Maintenance:

  • Easy Care: Requires minimal maintenance, making it suitable for both beginners and experienced aquarists.
  • Propagation: Propagates easily through division, allowing for quick and easy expansion of plant coverage.

Conclusion:

Red Root Floater (Phyllanthus fluitans) are an excellent choice for aquarists and pond keepers looking to add a splash of color and natural beauty to their water features. Their vibrant red roots and colorful leaves make them a standout addition to any aquatic environment. Easy to care for and highly beneficial for water quality, Red Root Floaters are perfect for creating a healthy and visually stunning aquatic landscape.

Enhance your aquarium or pond with the vibrant beauty and natural benefits of Red Root Floaters and enjoy the dynamic, colorful touch they bring to your aquatic setup.

  • Sold as a small portion
  • Approx. 2" x 2" in size (approx. 10 plants)

Disclaimer: Pictures are not the actual plants you will receive, but a sample representation. Unless specific, we don't guarantee aquatic plants will be free of pests, pesticides, grow emerged, or submerge.

 Phyllanthus Fluitans

Common Name

 Red Root Floaters

Plant Type

Placement

 Float on top of the water's surface

CO2

 Not required

Growth Rates

 Fast

Difficulty Level

Nutrient Substrate

 Not Required

Can it grow emerged?

 Yes with moist soil

products.title: Bucephalandra Pink Malawi
products.body_html:
🌸 Bucephalandra ‘Pink Malawi’ – Radiant Rose Jewel of Borneo

Bucephalandra ‘Pink Malawi’ is a captivating and rare Bucephalandra variety renowned for its delicate rose-pink to bronze iridescence and compact, graceful form. Originating from the crystal-clear streams of Borneo, this enchanting plant displays a soft metallic glow that shimmers beautifully under aquarium lighting—shifting between shades of pink, copper, and subtle violet. Slow-growing yet resilient, ‘Pink Malawi’ brings a gentle warmth and refined beauty to any aquascape, making it a must-have for collectors and aquascapers seeking a touch of elegance and color harmony.

🌟 Key Features

  • Scientific Name: Bucephalandra sp. ‘Pink Malawi’

  • Common Name: Buce Pink Malawi

  • Unique Coloration: Soft rose-pink to bronze hues with a pearlescent glow under high or RGB lighting.

  • Compact Growth Form: Naturally petite and neat, ideal for nano tanks or as a foreground accent.

  • Hardy & Adaptable: Thrives in a variety of aquarium conditions and lighting levels.

  • Epiphytic Nature: Easily attaches to driftwood, rocks, or other hardscape—keep the rhizome above substrate.

🌱 Growth & Propagation

  • Lighting Needs: Low to medium light; stronger light enhances pink and bronze coloration.

  • CO₂ & Fertilizers: Optional but beneficial for richer colors and faster growth.

  • Growth Method: Rhizome-based; attach gently to hardscape using thread or aquarium-safe glue.

  • Nutrient Substrate: Not required—absorbs nutrients directly from the water column.

  • Can Be Grown Emersed: Yes – an excellent choice for paludariums and humid terrarium displays.

🖼️ Aquascaping Benefits

  • Adds soft pink and copper tones that pair beautifully with green and dark-leaved Buce varieties.

  • Perfect for accenting focal points and enhancing contrast in planted aquariums.

  • Ideal for collector tanks, nano aquascapes, or refined color layering in nature layouts.

  • Creates a gentle, luxurious aesthetic that enhances the overall harmony of an aquascape.

📍 Cultivation & Sustainability

At Aquarium Plants Factory’s nursery, all of our Bucephalandra species are 100% farm-raised—never taken from the wild. Each plant originates ex-vitro (grown from sterile lab culture) before being transitioned to hydroponic growth in our controlled environment. This ensures exceptional quality, pest-free plants, and sustainable preservation of Borneo’s native ecosystems.

📦 What You’ll Receive

Sold as loose Bare-Root or Potted plants:

  • 1 Bare-Root: Approximately 6+ leaves

  • 5 Bare-Root: Approximately 25+ leaves total

  • 10 Bare-Root: Approximately 50+ leaves total

  • 1 Potted: Approximately 6+ leaves

  • 5 Potted: Approximately 25+ leaves total

  • 10 Potted: Approximately 50+ leaves total

⚠️ Disclaimer

Like most Bucephalandra species, minor leaf melt may occur after introduction as the plant adjusts to new water parameters. This is normal and temporary—new submersed leaves will regrow from the rhizome once the plant stabilizes.

🚫 State Restrictions Notice ♻️ Be a Responsible Aquatic Plants Owner

Certain aquatic plants may be restricted or regulated in some U.S. states. Always check your local and state regulations before purchasing or introducing plants into natural waterways. Never release aquarium plants into the wild.

products.title: Dracaena sanderiana 'Variegatus' – Variegated Lucky Bamboo
products.body_html:
🎋 Dracaena sanderiana 'Variegatus' – Variegated Lucky Bamboo

Dracaena sanderiana 'Variegatus', commonly known as Variegated Lucky Bamboo, is an elegant and low-maintenance plant that brings a touch of serenity and natural beauty to any space. Despite its common name, it’s not a true bamboo but a resilient species from the Dracaena family, prized for its striking green leaves edged with creamy white or yellow variegation.

Perfect for both aquariums and decorative indoor arrangements, this plant thrives in water or moist soil, making it ideal for homes, offices, or feng shui displays.

🌟 Key Features

  • Scientific Name: Dracaena sanderiana 'Variegatus'

  • Common Name: Variegated Lucky Bamboo

  • Appearance: Elegant green stalks with glossy leaves featuring creamy white margins

  • Versatile Growth: Can be grown in water, hydroponic setups, or soil

  • Symbolism: Represents luck, prosperity, and positive energy in feng shui culture

  • Low Maintenance: Ideal for beginners and busy plant owners

🌱 Growth & Care

  • Lighting: Prefers bright, indirect light but tolerates low-light conditions

  • Watering: Keep roots submerged in clean, chlorine-free water; change weekly

  • Temperature: Thrives in 65–90°F (18–32°C)

  • Humidity: Moderate to high humidity preferred

  • Substrate: Can grow in soil, pebbles, or water with proper support

  • Fertilization: Use a mild liquid fertilizer monthly during growing season

🖼️ Display Ideas

  • Perfect for modern home decor, office desks, or zen garden arrangements

  • Combine multiple stems in glass vases or decorative pots for a lush, elegant look

  • Ideal for feng shui placement to invite harmony and prosperity

📦 What You’ll Receive

Sold as fresh-cut, unrooted individual plants:
1 Plant: Approximately 10+ inches tall, ready for water or soil placement.

⚠️ Disclaimer

This plant is shipped fresh-cut and unrooted. Upon arrival, place the stem in clean, chlorine-free water to encourage root development. Avoid direct sunlight to prevent leaf burn.

products.title: Cryptocoryne Tonkinensis Vitro Culture
products.body_html:
Cryptocoryne crispatula 'Tonkinensis', also known as Cryptocoryne tonkinensis, is a beautiful aquatic plant that is highly sought after by aquarium enthusiasts. It is native to Southeast Asia and is commonly found in slow-moving streams and marshes. The plant has long, narrow leaves that can range in color from green to reddish-brown, depending on the lighting and nutrient levels in the water. Cryptocoryne crispatula 'Tonkinensis' is a relatively low-maintenance plant that can adapt to a wide range of water parameters, making it an excellent choice for both beginners and experienced hobbyists.

In addition to its ornamental value, Cryptocoryne crispatula 'Tonkinensis' also provides practical benefits to aquariums. It can help to absorb excess nutrients and provide shelter for aquatic inhabitants, creating a more balanced and healthy aquatic environment. Due to its unique appearance and practical benefits, Cryptocoryne crispatula 'Tonkinensis' has become a popular choice for aquascaping projects and is highly valued among aquatic plant collectors.

products.title: Limnophila Aromatica
products.body_html:
Limnophila Aromatica, also known as Rice Paddy Herb, is a highly prized aquarium plant known for its aromatic leaves and vibrant green to purple coloration. This versatile plant is perfect for adding texture and color to midground and background areas in your aquarium, making it a favorite among aquascaping enthusiasts.

Key Features

  • Scientific Name: Limnophila Aromatica
  • Common Name: Rice Paddy Herb
  • Family: Plantaginaceae
  • Leaf Color: Green to purple
  • Growth Form: Upright, bushy

Unique Characteristics

Limnophila Aromatica is renowned for its aromatic leaves that emit a pleasant scent when submerged. It originates from Southeast Asia, where it is commonly found in rice paddies and wet areas. This plant transitions from green to purple under high light conditions, creating a stunning visual effect in aquariums.

Ideal Conditions

To ensure the best growth and vibrant color for Limnophila Aromatica, provide the following conditions:

  • Light: Moderate to high intensity
  • CO2: Beneficial for optimal growth
  • Substrate: Nutrient-rich substrate preferred
  • Water Temperature: 20-28°C (68-82°F)
  • pH Range: 5.5-7.5

Care and Maintenance

This plant is relatively easy to care for and is suitable for both beginners and experienced aquarists:

  • Lighting: Ensure moderate to high light levels to maintain its vibrant coloration.
  • CO2 Injection: Regular CO2 supplementation can enhance growth and color intensity.
  • Trimming: Regular trimming helps maintain its form and encourages bushier growth.
  • Fertilization: Occasional fertilization supports vibrant growth and overall health.

Aquascaping Uses

Limnophila Aromatica is versatile and can be used in various ways:

  • Midground Plant: Ideal for adding a splash of green to purple in the midground area.
  • Background Plant: Can be used to create a lush, vibrant backdrop.
  • Accent Plant: Its unique color transition makes it an excellent focal point in aquascapes.

The green to purple leaves of Limnophila Aromatica provide excellent contrast and visual interest, making it a great choice for both Dutch and Nature-style aquascapes.

Propagation

Propagation of Limnophila Aromatica is straightforward. This plant propagates through stem cuttings. Simply cut a healthy stem and replant it in the substrate, where it will develop roots and grow into a new plant.

Limnophila Aromatica is a must-have for any aquarist looking to enhance their aquarium with a vibrant and aromatic plant. Its stunning color transition, aromatic leaves, and easy maintenance make it a standout choice for creating a beautiful, natural underwater landscape.

Enhance your aquarium with our versatile selection of high-quality aquatic plants, available in three convenient formats:

  • Fresh-Cut Plants: Includes approximately 6 loose, fresh-cut stems without roots. Perfect for flexible planting arrangements.
  • Fresh-Cut Bunched: Approximately 6 fresh-cut stems without roots, bundled with foam and a plant weight for easy placement.
  • Fresh-Cut Potted: About 6 fresh-cut stems without roots, securely potted in a net pot with rockwool, ensuring stability and healthy growth.

Choose the format that best suits your aquascaping needs and create a vibrant, lush underwater environment with our premium aquatic plants.

products.title: Bacopa Salzmannii
products.body_html:
🌸 Bacopa salzmannii sp. Araguaia (‘Pink’)

Color-shifting stem plant for vibrant aquascapes

Bacopa salzmannii sp. Araguaia (‘Pink’) is a striking stem plant prized for its dynamic color transformation and clean, rounded leaf form. Grown emersed at our Aquarium Plants Factory nursery, it shows green foliage; once submerged, it develops light green to light orange tones that can deepen toward pink to purple under stronger light and dialed-in nutrients. This is a rare collector’s variety with fast growth and refined texture that stands out in mid-ground and background groupings.

🌟 Key Features

  • Scientific Name: Bacopa salzmannii

  • Common Name: Bacopa salzmannii ‘Pink’ (Araguaia)

  • Color Shift: Emersed green ➝ submerged light green to light orange, pink, and purple highlights

  • Leaf Shape: Roundish, slightly elliptical leaves for a soft, premium look

  • Growth: Fast—readily forms lush bushes with routine trimming

  • Placement: Midground / Background

  • Rarity: Limited availability in the trade; a standout collector’s plant

🌱 Growth & Propagation

  • Difficulty: Advanced

  • Lighting: Medium – High (higher light = stronger pink/purple)

  • CO₂: Required for best color and compact growth

  • Nutrient Substrate: Required (root tabs or rich aquasoil recommended)

  • Water Parameters: 72–82 °F (22–28 °C), pH 6.0–7.5

  • Can Be Grown Emersed: Yes (emersed = green; submerged = color shift)

  • Propagation: Easy by tip and side cuttings; replant trimmed tops to thicken stands

  • Care Height (typical): 15–60 cm with trimming to shape

🖼️ Aquascaping Benefits

  • Vibrant contrast against green backgrounds; brings warm tones to nature and Dutch layouts

  • Rounded leaves add a softer texture between fine needles and broad swords

  • Fast growth fills space quickly; ideal for color “bands” or accent bushes

🔎 Variant Note

  • This Listing: Araguaia (‘Pink’)—emersed green; turns pink/purple after submersion.

  • Also Available Separately: Bacopa salzmannii ‘Purple’ (Singapore/“Deep Purple”)—notable for retaining purple tones even emersed. See our separate listing.

📦 What You’ll Receive

Sold as loose fresh-cut plants, bunched plants, or potted plants:

  • Fresh-Cut Plants: Approximately 6 individual, loose-cut stems without root

  • Bunched Plants: Approximately 6 stems without root bundled with foam and a plant weight

  • Potted Plants: Approximately 6 stems without root potted in rockwool and net pot

⚠️ Disclaimer
Pictures are representative. Color varies by lighting, CO₂, and nutrients. Some transit stress or leaf melt can occur; with stable conditions, fresh submerged growth rebounds quickly. Unless specified as tissue culture, we cannot guarantee plants are free of all pests or pesticides.

🚫 State Restrictions Notice ♻️ Be a Responsible Aquatic Plants Owner.
Certain aquatic plants may be restricted or regulated in some U.S. states. Always check your local and state regulations before purchasing or introducing plants into natural waterways. Never release aquarium plants into the wild.

products.title: Myriophyllum Elatinoides Vitro Culture
products.body_html:
Myriophyllum elatinoides in Vitro Culture® – Feather-Like Stem Plant for Flowing Aquascapes

Myriophyllum elatinoides is a graceful, fast-growing aquatic stem plant known for its soft, feather-like leaves and dynamic, bushy growth. Native to Central and South America, this plant creates a stunning, flowing effect in midground to background placements and is particularly prized in nature-style and Dutch-style aquascapes for its ability to add texture, contrast, and movement.

Now available in a Vitro Culture® 4-inch sterile bag, this plant is carefully grown in vitamin-enriched gel or liquid using advanced tissue culture techniques. Each portion yields multiple healthy plantlets and is 100% free from snails, algae, pathogens, and pesticides, making it ideal for shrimp tanks, nano aquariums, and clean aquascaping layouts.

🌿 Key Features:

  • Scientific Name: Myriophyllum elatinoides

  • Common Name: Parrot Feather or Red-Leaf Milfoil (in its emersed form)

  • Leaf Texture: Soft, feathery, whorled around the stem

  • Coloration: Bright green to reddish tones under high light

  • Vitro Culture® Format: 4-inch sterile bag with multiple plantlets

  • Placement: Midground to background

  • Growth Rate: Fast

  • Difficulty Level: Easy to moderate

💧 Optimal Growing Conditions:

  • Lighting: Medium to high (high light enhances red tones)

  • CO₂: Recommended for vigorous growth and coloration

  • Substrate: Nutrient-rich substrate or root tabs

  • Water Parameters: pH 6.0–7.5 | Temperature: 72–82°F (22–28°C)

  • Trimming: Frequent trimming needed to maintain shape and density

🧪 Vitro Culture® Advantages:

  • 100% Free from Snails, Algae, Pathogens, and Pesticides

  • Sterile Propagation: Perfect for shrimp-safe and pest-free aquascapes

  • Extended Shelf Life: Can be stored under moderate light before use

  • Ready to Plant: Rinse, separate, and plant directly into substrate

🐠 Aquascaping Benefits:

  • Delicate Texture: Adds flowing softness and natural movement to scapes

  • Color Accent: Transitions from green to red-orange in ideal conditions

  • Ideal for Shrimp Tanks: Safe, clean, and provides hiding areas

  • Dynamic Growth: Creates lush, full backgrounds or bushy midground zones

📦 What You’ll Receive:

One 4-inch sealed Vitro Culture® bag containing multiple Myriophyllum elatinoides plantlets, ready to be acclimated and planted in your aquarium.

⚠️ Disclaimer:

Photos are for reference only. Actual appearance and color may vary based on lighting and water conditions. Plant may melt slightly during transition from tissue culture to submerged life but will recover with proper care.

🚫Important Notice – Invasive Species Alert:

Attention! This plant is classified as an invasive species in several U.S. states. We do not ship to the following states:

Alabama, Colorado, Connecticut, Idaho, Illinois, Indiana, Maine, Massachusetts, Michigan, Montana, New Hampshire, New Mexico, New York, Ohio, Oregon, Vermont, Washington, and Wisconsin.

Please be a responsible aquatic plant owner. Never release any aquatic plant, animal, or life into the wild. Doing so can harm local ecosystems and violate environmental regulations.

products.title: Cryptocoryne Retrospiralis Vitro Culture
products.body_html:
🌿 Cryptocoryne Retrospiralis Vitro Culture®

Tall, Spiraling Leaves for Striking Background Effect — Grown in 4-inch Bag

Cryptocoryne retrospiralis is an elegant and distinctive background plant prized for its tall, ribbon-like leaves that exhibit beautiful spiraling patterns. Native to India, this classic species of Cryptocoryne adds vertical movement and texture to any aquascape. Under good lighting, the leaves can develop bronze to reddish hues, making it a versatile choice for both natural-style and Dutch-style aquariums.

Cultivated using our sterile Vitro Culture® method, each 4-inch bag is free from pests, algae, and pesticides—perfect for sensitive aquarium setups and shrimp tanks. This ensures you receive healthy, vibrant plants ready to thrive in your aquascape.

🌟 Key Features

  • Scientific Name: Cryptocoryne retrospiralis

  • Common Name: Retrospiralis Crypt, Cryptocoryne spiralis 'Red', Crypt Spiral Water Trumpet

  • Growth Form: Tall, spiraling ribbon-like leaves

  • Light Demand: Medium to high

  • CO₂ Requirement: Optional, but enhances coloration and growth

  • Growth Rate: Moderate

  • Placement: Background

  • Color: Green to bronze-red under high light

🌱 Growth & Propagation

  • Nutrient Substrate: Required — rich substrate promotes robust root development and vibrant leaf coloration

  • Can Be Grown Emersed: Yes — suitable for emersed or immersed culture, ideal for paludariums as well

Propagation: Easily propagated by runners; once established, the plant will send out new shoots around the mother plant.

🪴 What You’ll Receive

  • 1x Cryptocoryne Retrospiralis Vitro Culture® — grown in 4-inch bag

  • Yields approx. 3–4 healthy young plants ready for planting

🖼️ Aquascaping Benefits

  • Creates a graceful, flowing background that contrasts beautifully with compact foreground and midground plants

  • Adds height and dynamic movement to planted tanks

  • Ideal for nature aquariums and biotope setups

  • Safe for shrimp and sensitive livestock thanks to pesticide-free tissue culture process

⚠️ Disclaimer
Like many Cryptocoryne species, Cryptocoryne retrospiralis may undergo a temporary "Crypt melt" when adapting to a new aquarium environment. This is a normal process. Once established, new growth will emerge and the plant will stabilize.

⚠️ State Restrictions Notice ♻️ Be a Responsible Aquatic Plants Owner
Never release Cryptocoryne retrospiralis—or any aquarium plant—into natural waterways. Doing so can disrupt local ecosystems. Dispose of any unwanted portions responsibly to help preserve aquatic biodiversity.

products.title: Mini Pellia / Mini Coral Moss
products.body_html:
Riccardia Chamedryfolia – Rare Mini Moss with Coral-Like Texture

Riccardia chamedryfolia, commonly known as Mini Pellia, Mini Coral Moss, or Mini Liverwort, is a rare and visually stunning addition to any aquascape. Its intricate, branching fronds resemble tiny underwater coral, forming dense, cushion-like growths that bring texture and richness to foregrounds, hardscapes, or vertical moss walls.

Loved by aquascaping professionals and hobbyists alike, this miniature liverwort adds a refined elegance to nano tanks, shrimp aquariums, and planted layouts. Its slow, compact growth and rich green hue make it a standout feature in both high-tech and carefully managed low-tech setups.

🌿 Key Features of Riccardia Chamedryfolia

  • Scientific Name: Riccardia chamedryfolia

  • Common Names: Mini Pellia, Mini Coral Moss, Mini Liverwort

  • Appearance: Dark green, branching fronds resembling fine coral structures

  • Growth Habit: Compact, moss-like cushions with slow, controlled growth

  • Versatile Use: Ideal for attaching to rocks, driftwood, mesh, or hardscape elements

✅ Optimal Growing Conditions

  • Lighting: Moderate to high recommended for best coloration and growth

  • CO₂: Strongly recommended for healthy development and dense form

  • Nutrient Substrate: Not required but benefits from water column fertilizers

  • Water Parameters:

    • pH: 6.0–7.4

    • Temperature: 64–75°F (18–24°C)

🐠 Aquascaping Benefits

  • Distinct Visual Texture: Delivers a unique coral-like appearance that contrasts beautifully with finer mosses and broad-leaf plants

  • Microhabitat Creation: Offers safe refuge and grazing surfaces for shrimp, fry, and microfauna

  • High Design Versatility: Can be used in fine detail work or as a statement mat in nano aquariums

  • Stable & Controlled Growth: Low maintenance and doesn’t require frequent trimming

🌱 Why Choose Riccardia Chamedryfolia?

If you’re looking to elevate your aquascape with a rare and elegant texture, Mini Pellia is the perfect plant. Its coral-like structure, dark green tone, and sculptural form make it a designer’s favorite for modern and nature-style aquariums. With a little patience and care, it forms a lasting, natural feature that transforms your layout into a serene underwater haven.

📦 What You’ll Receive

  • Loose Portion: approximately 2" x 2" in size

  • Perfect for Attachment: Can be tied or glued to stones, driftwood, or used on mesh for walls or carpets

🔒 Disclaimer

Images shown are sample representations. Actual plants may vary in size, density, or appearance. We do not guarantee delivery in submerged or emersed form unless noted. Plants may carry natural variation and are not guaranteed to be pest- or pesticide-free.

⚠️ State Restrictions Notice ♻️ Be a Responsible Aquatic Plants Owner

Please do not release Riccardia chamedryfolia—or any aquatic plant—into local waterways. Doing so may disrupt native ecosystems. Dispose of unused plant material responsibly.

products.title: Amazon Sword Mother Plant
products.body_html:
Amazon Sword (Echinodorus bleheri) is one of the most popular and versatile aquatic plants among aquarists. Known for its lush, broad leaves and vibrant green color, this plant is a perfect choice for creating a natural and aesthetically pleasing underwater landscape.

Key Features:

  • Robust Growth: Amazon Sword is a hardy plant that can thrive in a variety of aquarium conditions. Its robust growth makes it an ideal choice for beginners and experienced aquarists alike.
  • Visual Appeal: With its large, sword-shaped leaves, Amazon Sword adds a dramatic and natural look to any aquarium. It serves as an excellent background plant, providing shelter and hiding spots for fish.
  • Low Maintenance: This plant is easy to care for and requires minimal maintenance. It can adapt to a wide range of lighting conditions, though it thrives best under moderate to high light.

Optimal Growing Conditions:

  • Lighting: Amazon Sword prefers moderate to high lighting to promote healthy growth and vibrant coloration.
  • CO2 and Nutrients: While not strictly necessary, CO2 supplementation and regular fertilization with macro and micronutrients can enhance growth and overall health.
  • Water Parameters: This plant is adaptable to various water conditions but prefers slightly acidic to neutral pH (6.5-7.5) and temperatures between 22-28°C (72-82°F).

Benefits:

  • Aesthetic Enhancement: The lush, green foliage of Amazon Sword enhances the visual appeal of any aquarium, creating a natural and serene environment.
  • Habitat Creation: Provides excellent cover and hiding spots for fish, promoting a more secure and natural habitat.
  • Versatility: Suitable for a range of aquascaping styles, from beginner setups to intricate, high-tech aquariums.

Conclusion:

Amazon Sword (Echinodorus bleheri) is a must-have for any aquarium enthusiast. Its robust growth, striking appearance, and ease of care make it an ideal choice for enhancing the beauty and functionality of your aquarium. Whether you're a beginner or an experienced aquarist, Amazon Sword will bring life and vibrancy to your underwater world.

products.title: Vallisneria Contortionist
products.body_html:
Vallisneria Contortionist, also known as Corkscrew Val, Contortion Val, or Twisted Val, is a striking and versatile addition to any freshwater aquarium. With its unique twisted leaves and impressive height, this plant brings a dynamic, natural aesthetic to aquatic landscapes, making it a favorite among aquarists.

Key Features:

  • Unique Appearance: Features narrow, twisted leaves that grow straight upward, adding texture and movement to your aquarium.
  • Tall Growth: Reaches heights of 20 inches or more, making it an excellent background plant to create depth and dimension.
  • Rapid Propagation: Easily propagates through runners, quickly filling in large spaces with lush greenery.

Optimal Conditions:

  • Substrate: Thrives in sandy or soil substrates, which promote healthy growth and efficient reproduction via runners.
  • Lighting: Performs well under moderate to high lighting, enhancing its vibrant green color and overall growth rate.
  • Water Parameters: Adapts to a wide range of water conditions but prefers slightly alkaline to neutral pH levels (6.5–8.0) and temperatures between 72°F to 82°F (22°C to 28°C).

Aquarium Benefits:

Conclusion:

Vallisneria Contortionist (Corkscrew Val) is an excellent choice for aquarists looking to enhance their aquarium with a tall, distinctive background plant. Its twisted leaves and fast propagation make it ideal for creating a lush, natural underwater environment while improving water quality.

products.title: Rotala ramosior 'Florida'
products.body_html:
Rotala ramosior 'Florida' – Purple-Toned Freshwater Stem Plant

Rotala ramosior 'Florida' is a rare and visually captivating aquatic plant prized for its vibrant purple tones when grown submersed in planted aquariums. Native to North America and selectively cultivated for its color intensity, this variety of Rotala offers aquarists a stunning alternative to traditional green plants. Under the right conditions, Rotala ramosior 'Florida' develops rich lavender to deep purple hues—providing a bold pop of color that enhances the depth and vibrancy of any aquascape.

🌿 Key Features & Benefits

  • Scientific Name: Rotala ramosior 'Florida'

  • Common Name: Florida Rotala

  • Color-Changing Beauty: Displays a striking purple hue under high lighting and CO₂-enriched conditions

  • Fine, Delicate Growth: Narrow leaves grow in opposite pairs along vertical stems, creating elegant structure

  • Moderate Growth Rate: Easily manageable with occasional trimming

  • Lighting Requirements: Medium to high lighting encourages optimal color development

  • CO₂ and Fertilizer: CO₂ injection and nutrient-rich water or substrate improve color and density

  • Propagation: Simple stem cuttings—clip healthy tops and replant directly in substrate

  • Tank Placement: Ideal for midground to background in high-tech aquascapes, nature aquariums, and Dutch-style layouts

💧 Why Rotala ramosior 'Florida' is a Must-Have

For aquarists looking to add vibrant coloration and vertical interest to their freshwater aquarium, Rotala ramosior 'Florida' offers an elegant solution. Its distinctive purple tones provide a stunning contrast to green aquatic plants, creating a dynamic visual effect that elevates the entire aquascape. Whether you're designing a nature aquarium, a color-focused Dutch-style layout, or simply want to add a unique species to your collection, this plant delivers both aesthetic appeal and ecological benefits.

Its moderate growth rate makes it easy to maintain, and its propagation through stem cuttings allows for effortless expansion throughout your tank. While it thrives best with CO₂ supplementation and strong lighting, it remains relatively easy to care for—making it suitable for both intermediate hobbyists and advanced aquascapers.

✅ Conclusion

Rotala ramosior 'Florida' is a rare and striking addition to any planted aquarium, offering brilliant purple coloration, graceful structure, and ease of propagation. With the right conditions, it becomes a showpiece plant that adds depth, color, and elegance to your underwater landscape.

products.title: Water Lettuce
products.body_html:
🌿 Water Lettuce (Pistia stratiotes) | Floating Aquarium & Pond Plant

Water Lettuce (Pistia stratiotes) is a classic floating plant known for its beautiful, rosette-shaped leaves that resemble small heads of lettuce. Its soft, velvety foliage and long dangling roots provide excellent cover and natural filtration for ponds and aquariums. Water Lettuce thrives in calm, nutrient-rich waters, helping to shade your aquatic environment and reduce algae growth.

🌟 Key Features:

  • Natural Filtration: Absorbs excess nutrients, improving water quality.

  • Floating Growth: Perfect for ponds, water gardens, and outdoor tubs.

  • Aesthetic Appeal: Velvety green leaves form charming rosettes atop the water surface.

  • Fish & Wildlife Benefits: Provides shade and protection for fish, frogs, and other aquatic life.

🌱 Optimal Growing Conditions:

  • Lighting: Medium to high; prefers full sun in outdoor ponds.

  • Water Parameters: pH 6.0–7.5; temperature 70–85°F (21–29°C)

  • Placement: Floating on the water surface; no planting needed.

🖼️ Aquascaping Benefits:

  • Reduces algae by blocking light and absorbing excess nutrients.

  • Creates a natural, lush surface for ponds and outdoor water features.

  • Provides refuge for fry, shrimp, and aquatic critters.

📦 What You’ll Receive:

Sold as a small portion of loose,  Lettuce rosettes (approximately 1–2 plants, depending on size and season).

⚠️ Disclaimer:

Water Lettuce is sensitive to frost and should not be used in outdoor settings during freezing temperatures. In tropical and subtropical climates, this plant can spread rapidly. Always monitor and maintain responsibly.

🚫 State Restrictions Notice ♻️ Be a Responsible Aquatic Plants Owner

We will NOT ship Water Lettuce (Pistia stratiotes) to the following states or territories due to legal restrictions:

Alabama (AL), California (CA), Florida (FL), Louisiana (LA), Mississippi (MS), South Carolina (SC), Texas (TX), Wisconsin (WI), Ohio (OH), Maine (ME), Puerto Rico (PR).

Please respect local regulations and do not release this plant into natural waterways. Always follow state laws and practice responsible plant care.

products.title: Cabomba Furcata
products.body_html:
Cabomba Furcata (Red Cabomba), also known as Fanwort or Cabomba Plant, is a striking aquatic plant that adds a vibrant splash of color and texture to freshwater aquariums. With its delicate, feathery leaves and stunning pink, reddish-purple hues, this plant creates a beautiful contrast against green foliage, enhancing the overall aesthetic of your aquascape.

Key Features:

  • Vibrant Red Coloration: Displays eye-catching reddish-purple leaves under optimal lighting, making it a standout feature in any aquarium.
  • Feathery Appearance: The finely divided, fan-like leaves create a soft and elegant look, perfect for background planting or as a midground accent.
  • Rapid Growth: A fast-growing plant that helps absorb excess nutrients, improving water quality and reducing algae growth.

Optimal Conditions:

  • Lighting: Requires moderate to high lighting to achieve its vibrant red coloration. Stronger lighting enhances its reddish hues.
  • Water Parameters: Thrives in slightly acidic to neutral water with a pH range of 6.0-7.5 and temperatures between 72°F to 82°F (22°C to 28°C).
  • Nutrients: Benefits from nutrient-rich substrate and liquid fertilizers to support healthy growth. CO2 supplementation enhances its color and vitality but is not strictly necessary.

Aquarium Benefits:

  • Natural Habitat: Provides shelter and spawning areas for fish and shrimp, promoting a more natural and stress-free environment.
  • Improves Water Quality: Absorbs excess nitrates and other nutrients, helping to maintain a balanced aquarium ecosystem.
  • Aesthetic Appeal: Adds height, movement, and a vibrant red focal point to your aquascape, making it ideal for background planting in larger tanks.

Care and Maintenance:

  • Easy to Moderate Care: While relatively undemanding, it thrives best in well-lit setups with a nutrient-rich environment.
  • Pruning: Trim regularly to maintain shape and encourage bushy, compact growth. Plant cuttings can easily propagate to create new plants.

Conclusion:

Cabomba Furcata (Red Cabomba) is the perfect choice for aquarists looking to elevate their aquarium with a splash of red and a touch of elegance. Its vibrant color, delicate texture, and fast growth make it a versatile and visually stunning addition to freshwater aquascapes. Whether used as a background feature or a midground accent, this plant is sure to enhance the beauty and balance of your underwater world.

Bring home Cabomba Furcata today and transform your aquarium into a lush, vibrant aquatic paradise!

products.title: Hyptis Lorentziana (Purple Mint)
products.body_html:
Hyptis Lorentziana, commonly known as Purple Mint, is a rare and captivating aquarium plant known for its vibrant purple-red leaves. This plant is ideal for adding a unique touch of color to the background areas in your aquarium, making it a favorite among aquascaping enthusiasts who seek rare and exotic flora.

Key Features

  • Scientific Name: Hyptis Lorentziana
  • Common Name: Purple Mint
  • Family: Lamiaceae
  • Leaf Color: Deep purple-red
  • Growth Form: Moderate height with bushy growth

Unique Characteristics

Hyptis Lorentziana has been a subject of intrigue in the aquatic plant hobby. It was originally called "Hemigraphis traian" but was correctly identified in late 2009 as belonging to the family Lamiaceae. Native to South America, it thrives on the edges of ponds and wet areas, growing up to 2 meters tall in natural settings. This plant is rare in trade but is gaining popularity among hobbyists worldwide.

Ideal Conditions

To ensure the best growth and vibrant color for Hyptis Lorentziana, provide the following conditions:

  • Light: Strong lighting required
  • CO2: Beneficial for optimal growth
  • Substrate: Nutrient-rich substrate preferred
  • Water Temperature: 20-28°C (68-82°F)
  • pH Range: 6.0-7.5
  • Nitrate Level: Around 10 mg/l
  • Phosphate Level: 1-2 mg/l

Care and Maintenance

This plant is relatively easy to care for and is suitable for both beginners and experienced aquarists:

  • Lighting: Ensure strong light levels to maintain its vibrant purple-red coloration.
  • CO2 Injection: Regular CO2 supplementation can enhance growth and color intensity.
  • Trimming: Regular trimming helps maintain its form and encourages bushier growth.
  • Fertilization: Regular fertilization supports vibrant growth and overall health.

Aquascaping Uses

Hyptis Lorentziana is versatile and can be used in various ways:

  • Background Plant: Ideal for adding a unique touch of purple-red to the background area.
  • Accent Plant: Its intense color makes it an excellent focal point in aquascapes.

The large purple-red leaves of Hyptis Lorentziana provide excellent contrast and visual interest, making it a great choice for both Dutch and Nature-style aquascapes.

Propagation

Propagation of Hyptis Lorentziana is straightforward. This plant propagates through stem cuttings. Cut a healthy stem directly above a leaf pair, around 8 to 10 cm above the ground, and replant it in the substrate. This encourages new growth and lateral shoot formation.

Hyptis Lorentziana is a must-have for any aquarist looking to enhance their aquarium with a vibrant and eye-catching plant. Its stunning purple-red leaves, moderate growth, and easy maintenance make it a standout choice for creating a beautiful, natural underwater landscape.

Enhance your aquarium with our versatile selection of high-quality aquatic plants, available in three convenient formats:

  • Fresh-Cut Bunched: Approximately 3+ fresh-cut stems without roots, bundled with foam and a plant weight for easy placement.
  • Fresh-Cut Potted: About 3+ fresh-cut stems without roots, securely potted in a net pot with rockwool, ensuring stability and healthy growth.

Choose the format that best suits your aquascaping needs and create a vibrant, lush underwater environment with our premium aquatic plants.

products.title: Vallisneria nana 'Striped'
products.body_html:
🌿 Vallisneria nana 'Striped' – Hardy, Patterned Background Grass Plant

Vallisneria nana 'Striped', also known as Vallisneria nana 'Tiger', is a robust and undemanding background plant ideal for planted aquariums. This cultivar is a hybrid derived from Vallisneria nana, combining its narrow-leaved form with subtle variegation in the form of brown striping or speckled dot patterns on some leaves. Known for its hardiness, it is even more resilient than Vallisneria spiralis and can quickly propagate via runners to form dense, swaying backgrounds.

Reaching over 20 inches in height under optimal conditions, this Tiger Vallisneria retains its rich green coloration even under high-intensity lighting—unlike its cousin Vallisneria spiralis 'Leopard', which develops intense red hues and a more marbled texture. Its graceful, upright growth and ease of care make it a standout choice for aquascapers of all skill levels.

🌟 Key Features:

  • Scientific Name: Vallisneria nana 'Striped'

  • Synonyms: Vallisneria nana 'Tiger'

  • Common Name: Tiger Vallisneria

  • Subtle Leaf Variegation: May display random brown stripes or speckles on green leaves

  • Hardy and Adaptable: More resilient than Vallisneria spiralis

  • Tall Growth: Can reach 20+ inches, great for background coverage

  • Runners for Propagation: Spreads easily to create lush plant groupings

🌱 Growth & Propagation:

  • Lighting Needs: Moderate to high light

  • CO₂ & Fertilizers: CO₂ not required; nutrient-rich substrate or root tabs recommended

  • Growth Method: Grows vertically and propagates rapidly via runners

  • Nutrient Substrate: Recommended

  • Can Be Grown Emersed: Yes

🖼️ Aquascaping Benefits:

  • Creates soft, natural movement in the background

  • Ideal for Dutch-style and jungle-style aquascapes

  • Pairs well with red or colorful stem plants for contrast

  • Helps soften hardscape transitions and background lines

📦 What You’ll Receive:

Sold as loose bare-root plants, bunched plants, or potted plants:

  • Bare-root Plants: Approximately 2–3 individual loose, bare-root plants

  • Bunched Plants: Approximately 2–3 individual bare-root plants bundled with foam and a plant weight

  • Potted Plants: Approximately 2–3 individual bare-root plants potted in rockwool and net pot

⚠️ Disclaimer:

Some leaf melting may occur when transitioning to a new tank. Stripe intensity and patterning may vary between individual plants. Provide stable lighting and substrate for best performance.

Always follow your local guidelines and never release aquarium plants into natural water systems. Dispose of plant trimmings responsibly.

products.title: Vallisneria Torta
products.body_html:
This pretty, small corkscrew Vallisneria is labeled by several water plant nurseries as 'Vallisneria Torta'. It is probably Vallisneria asiatica var. biwaensis, but as long as its identity is unclear, we offer the plant as Vallisneria Torta. According to descriptions by the nurseries, it is the smallest Vallisneria, reaching a height of just 20 cm. The leaves are light green, somewhat transparent, and spirally twisted.

As well as most other Vallisneria, Vallisneria Torta has no special demands. Moderate light intensity is sufficient. The plant propagates itself by runners. If it spreads too much, newly appearing runners can be removed.

Vallisneria sp. "Torta" looks best as a group in the midground or foreground and can also be used for nano aquariums. With its spiral, light green leaves it provides an interesting accent.

products.title: Hygrophila Corymbosa
products.body_html:
Hygrophila Corymbosa, commonly known as Giant Hygro, Temple Plant, Nomaphila Corymbosa, and Hygrophila Stricta, is a versatile and attractive aquatic plant. It boasts hardy stems with large, broad, vibrant green leaves that add elegance and lushness to any aquarium setup. Ideal for both novice and experienced aquarists, Hygrophila Corymbosa is renowned for its hardiness and ease of care.

Key Features:

  • Broad Green Leaves: Creates a stunning visual impact with its large, lush foliage.
  • Fast Growth Rate: Quickly fills in the midground or background, providing a natural, dense look.
  • Hardy and Adaptable: Thrives in various water conditions and light levels.
  • Dutch Style Aquascape: Often used in Dutch style aquascapes for its striking appearance and ability to create depth.
  • Versatile Planting: Suitable for midground and background placement in a wide range of aquascape styles.

Care Instructions:

  • Light Requirement: Medium to high light for optimal growth.
  • CO2 Supplementation: Beneficial but not essential.
  • Optimal Temperature: 72°F to 82°F (22°C to 28°C).
  • pH Range: 6.0 to 7.5.

Trimming and Propagation:

  • Trimming: Regular trimming is crucial to maintain the plant's shape and prevent it from overshadowing other plants. Use sharp scissors to cut back the stems to the desired height, encouraging bushier growth.
  • Propagation: Propagate Hygrophila Corymbosa easily through stem cuttings. Cut a healthy stem with several leaves and replant it in the substrate. The new cutting will quickly develop roots and start growing.

products.title: Limnophila Heterophylla
products.body_html:
Limnophila Heterophylla, a striking yet easy-to-care-for aquatic plant, is a fantastic addition to any freshwater aquarium. Originating from Southeast Asia, it is renowned for its fast growth and feathery, vibrant green leaves, making it a favorite among aquascaping enthusiasts.

This plant is highly adaptable, thriving in a range of water conditions and light levels, though it flourishes best in medium to high lighting. Its rapid growth rate makes it an excellent choice for filling in the background or midground of tanks, creating a lush, dense aquatic landscape. Limnophila Heterophylla is also known for its ability to absorb excess nutrients, helping to maintain water quality and reduce algae growth.

Propagation is simple and efficient; just trim the top of the plant and replant the cutting into the substrate. This process allows for easy cultivation and shaping of your underwater garden.

Limnophila Heterophylla is not only aesthetically pleasing but also creates a healthy environment for your fish and invertebrates, providing shelter and enhancing the overall biodiversity of your aquarium.

Whether you're a seasoned aquarist or just starting, Limnophila Heterophylla is an excellent choice for adding beauty and balance to your aquatic setup.

products.title: Ludwigia Red Skeleton
products.body_html:
Ludwigia Rubin 'Red Skeleton' commonly known as Ludwigia Red Skeleton. It is a rare and captivating mutation of Ludwigia repens 'Rubin' perfect for adding a unique touch to any planted aquarium. This aquatic plant is notable for its red stems, red under-leaves, and green upper-leaves with yellow-striped variegation in a striking skeleton pattern. Its distinctive beauty sets it apart, making it a standout choice for aquascaping enthusiasts. Often confused with Ludwigia sp. Atlantis which is a mutation of Ludwigia Repens. 

Key Features:

  • Distinctive Appearance: Features red stems, red under-leaves, and green upper-leaves with yellow-striped variegation in an intricate skeleton pattern.
  • Color Variation: Exhibits stunning deep red coloration under high lighting, while the leaves remain green in low light conditions.
  • Stable Mutation: Offspring consistently inherit the variegated genes, ensuring reliable coloration and patterning.

Ideal Conditions:

  • Lighting and Water Parameters: Thrives in moderate to high lighting. In low light, the leaves stay green; in high light, they turn a striking red. Prefers slightly acidic to neutral pH levels (6.5 to 7.5), with temperatures between 72°F to 82°F (22°C to 28°C).
  • Submerged Growth: Transforms into a beautiful deep red when grown submerged, adding elegance and vibrancy to your aquatic environment.

Aquascaping Benefits:

  • Eye-Catching Focal Point: The unique coloration and pattern make it a perfect centerpiece, drawing attention in any aquarium.
  • Versatile Planting Options: Can be used singly for a striking focal point or planted in groups for a dynamic and interesting background.

Care and Maintenance:

  • Easy to Grow: Adaptable and low-maintenance, making it suitable for both novice and experienced aquarists.
  • Lighting Requirements: Readily turns red under moderate lighting conditions, enhancing the visual appeal of your aquarium.

Conclusion: Ludwigia Rubin 'Red Skeleton' is a must-have for aquarists looking to add a touch of rare beauty and elegance to their freshwater aquariums. At Aquarium Plants Factory, we take pride in successfully cultivating this extraordinary species and we are the first aquatic plant nursery in the United States to release this plant to the market. The stable mutation ensures that offspring inherit the variegated genes from their parents, guaranteeing consistency and quality in every plant. Its impressive coloration, easy growth, and stable mutation make it an ideal choice for creating stunning aquascapes. Whether used as a focal point or a complementary background plant, Ludwigia Rubin 'Red Skeleton' enhances the visual appeal and ecological balance of any aquarium.

products.title: Bolbitis Heudelotii (African Water Fern)
products.body_html:
Bolbitis heudelotii, also known as African water fern or Congo fern, is a species of aquatic fern found in Africa. It is a popular plant in the aquarium hobby due to its attractive appearance and ease of care.

Bolbitis heudelotii has a rhizomatous growth habit, meaning it spreads by sending out horizontal stems called rhizomes. It typically grows to a height of 10-20 inches (25-50 cm) and has long, narrow leaves that can reach up to 18 inches (45 cm) in length. The leaves are dark green in color and have a glossy texture.

In the aquarium, Bolbitis heudelotii can be grown attached to rocks or driftwood, or planted directly in the substrate. It prefers low to moderate lighting and a neutral pH range of 6.5-7.5. It is a slow-growing plant that requires little maintenance, but will benefit from occasional fertilization and CO2 supplementation.

Bolbitis heudelotii is also found in the wild in streams and rivers in tropical Africa, where it serves an important ecological role as a primary producer and as a habitat for fish and other aquatic organisms.

products.body_html:
🌿 Vallisneria nana – Graceful, Thin-Leaved Background Plant

Vallisneria nana, also known as Vallisneria gracilis, is a slender, grass-like aquatic plant perfect for adding natural movement and vertical texture to any freshwater aquarium. Native to Australia, this species features narrow, ribbon-like leaves that gently sway with the water flow, creating a serene and dynamic aquascape. Unlike other Vallisneria species, Vallisneria nana stays more compact and manageable, making it a preferred choice for nano tanks and smaller layouts.

  • Synonyms: Vallisneria gracilis

  • Background Favorite: Ideal for background planting in nano to medium-sized aquariums.

  • Low Maintenance: Hardy and beginner-friendly with a wide tolerance for water conditions.

products.title: Cuphea Anagalloidea
products.body_html:
Cuphea anagalloidea is a delicate small stem plant that requires a lot of care.
The brilliant shade of scarlet that may be found on the upper surface of the submerged leaves is brought out to its fullest potential when exposed to bright light. This plant was initially introduced into the aquarium hobby under the name Rotala sp. Araguaia; however, it was later determined that it belongs to the Cuphea species. Cuphea Anagalloidea can only grow vertically. It looks best when used in a group in the middleground as a color accent. The upper edges of the leaves turn a bright crimson to orange-red color under high lighting, but the stem remains green. Sufficient lighting, with plenty CO2 and nutrients, and soft water are all recommended.

products.title: Ceratopteris Siliquosa
products.body_html:
Ceratopteris Siliquosa, affectionately known as Water Sprite, is a fantastic choice for any aquarium enthusiast. This versatile and fast-growing plant not only adds a lush green touch to your tank but also helps in maintaining a healthy aquatic environment. Whether you're new to the hobby or an experienced aquarist, Water Sprite is an excellent addition to your aquarium setup.

Key Features of Ceratopteris Siliquosa (Water Sprite)

  1. Beautiful Fern-Like Leaves: Water Sprite is prized for its delicate, lace-like leaves that create a light, airy look. The fresh green foliage brings a touch of nature's elegance into your tank, making it a visual treat.

  2. Versatile Growing Options: Whether you want it floating on the water surface or anchored in the substrate, Water Sprite adapts beautifully. This flexibility allows for creative aquascaping, letting you design a tank that's uniquely yours.

  3. Rapid Growth: Known for its fast growth, Water Sprite quickly fills in gaps, providing cover and helping to keep algae at bay by absorbing excess nutrients. It's a great plant for those looking to establish a lush aquarium in no time.

  4. Natural Habitat: Originating from tropical regions worldwide, Water Sprite thrives in warm, freshwater environments. Its ability to adapt to different conditions makes it ideal for tropical biotope aquariums.

  5. Easy Propagation: Growing your collection of Water Sprite is simple. New plants sprout from the leaves, making it easy to propagate and share with friends or spread throughout your tank.

Care Tips for Ceratopteris Siliquosa (Water Sprite)

  • Lighting: Moderate to high lighting is best for Water Sprite, ensuring robust growth and vibrant green leaves.
  • Water Conditions: This adaptable plant prefers temperatures between 68-82°F (20-28°C) and a pH range of 6.0 to 7.5. It thrives in various water conditions, making it a resilient choice.
  • Substrate: While Water Sprite can float, it also does well anchored in the substrate. A nutrient-rich substrate is beneficial but not essential since it absorbs nutrients from the water.
  • Fertilization: Regular use of a balanced aquarium fertilizer promotes healthy growth. CO2 supplementation isn't necessary but can enhance growth.
  • Water Flow: Moderate water flow keeps the leaves clean and simulates its natural environment, helping to prevent debris accumulation.

Benefits of Adding Ceratopteris Siliquosa (Water Sprite) to Your Aquarium

  • Stunning Visuals: The fern-like leaves of Water Sprite add a striking natural element to your tank, creating a serene and attractive underwater landscape.
  • Habitat Enrichment: It provides excellent cover for fish and invertebrates, making your aquarium a more dynamic and engaging environment.
  • Improved Water Quality: Water Sprite helps maintain a clean tank by absorbing excess nutrients and oxygenating the water, reducing the likelihood of algae growth.

Ceratopteris Siliquosa (Water Sprite) is an outstanding choice for anyone looking to enhance their aquarium with lush greenery and improve the overall health of their aquatic environment. Its rapid growth, ease of care, and beautiful appearance make it a standout addition to any tank.

products.title: Ludwigia sp. White
products.body_html:
Ludwigia inclinata var. verticillata 'White', commonly known as Ludwigia sp. White or Ludwigia sp. Pink, is a stunning and versatile aquatic plant that thrives in high-tech planted aquariums. This plant is known for its delicate, pink, and white leaves that add a touch of elegance and color to any aquascape.

Key Features:

  • Striking Appearance: Features delicate leaves that develop beautiful pink and white hues when grown submerged, creating a visually appealing contrast in your aquarium.
  • Versatile Growth: Can be grown both emersed and submerged, adapting its leaf structure accordingly.

Optimal Conditions:

  • High Light: Requires high lighting conditions to maintain its vibrant coloration and healthy growth.
  • CO2 Supplementation: Benefits significantly from ample CO2 supply, promoting robust and vibrant growth.
  • Nutrient-Rich Substrate: Thrives in soil-based nutrient substrates that provide essential nutrients for optimal development.
  • Natural Sunlight: Grows best with plenty of natural sunlight, making it ideal for aquariums with good natural light exposure.

Aquarium Benefits:

  • Aesthetic Appeal: Adds a unique and colorful element to aquascapes with its pink and white leaves, enhancing the overall visual appeal of the aquarium.
  • Versatile Placement: Suitable for midground or background placement, providing depth and contrast in high-tech planted aquariums.

Care and Maintenance:

  • Delicate Handling: Handle with care when transitioning from emersed to submerged growth to avoid damaging the delicate leaves.
  • Regular Trimming: Regular trimming helps maintain its shape and encourages bushier growth.
  • Consistent Conditions: Ensure stable water quality, CO2 levels, and nutrient availability for the best growth results.

Conclusion:

Ludwigia inclinata var. verticillata 'White' (Ludwigia sp. White/Pink) is an excellent choice for aquarists looking to add a touch of elegance and vibrant color to their high-tech planted aquariums. Its delicate pink and white leaves and unique growth patterns make it a standout addition to any aquascape. With proper care, including high light, CO2 supplementation, and a nutrient-rich substrate, this plant will thrive and transform your aquarium into a stunning underwater garden.

Enhance your aquarium with the captivating beauty of Ludwigia inclinata var. verticillata 'White' and enjoy the lush, vibrant transformation it brings to your aquatic environment.

products.title: Microsorum Pteropus Trident
products.body_html:
🌿 Microsorum pteropus ‘Trident’ – Delicate and Elegant Java Fern with Forked Leaves

Microsorum pteropus 'Trident', commonly known as Java Fern Trident, is a stunning and refined cultivar of the classic Java Fern, instantly recognizable by its narrow, deeply forked leaves. Resembling the prongs of a trident, each leaf splits into multiple lobes, creating a soft, flowing texture that adds intricate detail and movement to any aquascape.

At Aquarium Plants Factory, we grow Trident in its emersed form, promoting strong rhizome development and compact leaf growth. Once submerged, it transitions beautifully, producing lush green fronds that sway gently in the current. Its fine texture makes it an excellent choice for midground placement or even in the background of nano tanks, offering elegance without overwhelming the space.

Like all Microsorum species, Java Fern Trident is a rhizome plant that must be attached to driftwood, rocks, or hardscape, and should never be buried in substrate. It is extremely low maintenance, thrives in low to medium light, and requires no CO₂—making it perfect for aquarists of all experience levels.

🌟 Key Features:

  • Trident-Shaped Leaves: Narrow leaves with multiple lobes for a delicate, airy appearance

  • Attachable Rhizome: Mount to hardscape like driftwood or stone—do not bury in substrate

  • Low-Tech Friendly: Grows well without CO₂ and tolerates low to medium light

  • Elegant Movement: Soft fronds sway naturally with water flow, adding visual interest

  • Great for All Levels: Hardy, slow-growing, and easy to care for

🔎 Botanical Information:

  • Scientific Name: Microsorum pteropus ‘Trident’

  • Synonyms: Leptochilus pteropus ‘Trident’

  • Common Names: Java Fern Trident

  • Plant Type: Rhizome plant

  • Placement: Attach to driftwood, stone, or hardscape

  • Growth Rate: Slow

  • Lighting: Low to Medium

  • Origin: Southeast Asia

💡 Optimal Growing Conditions:

  • Lighting: Performs well under low to medium lighting

  • CO₂ & Fertilization: Not required; optional fertilization helps maintain vibrancy

  • Water Parameters:

    • pH: 6.0–7.5

    • Temperature: 68–82°F (20–28°C)

    • Water Hardness: Soft to moderately hard

🌱 Growth & Propagation:

  • CO₂ Requirement: Not required

  • Nutrient Substrate: Not required

  • Can Grow Emersed: Yes

  • Propagation Method: Rhizome division

📦 What You’ll Receive:

Sold as loose bare-root plants, bunched plants, or potted plants:

  • Bare-root Plants: Approximately 3+ leaf portion, loose rhizome

  • Bunched Plants: Approximately 3+ leaf portion bundled with foam and plant weight

  • Potted Plants: Approximately 3+ leaf portion, potted in rockwool and net pot

⚠️ Disclaimer:

Photos are sample representations. Actual plants may vary in size, shape, or coloration. Unless otherwise specified, we do not guarantee plants are free of pests, pesticides, or exclusively submerged-grown.

products.title: Vallisneria Americana
products.body_html:
🌿 Vallisneria americana 'Asiatica' – Twisting Corkscrew Val for Flowing Foreground & Midground Texture

Vallisneria americana 'Asiatica', also known as Corkscrew Vallisneria or Twisted Val, is a unique and eye-catching variety of Vallisneria with narrow, bright green leaves that twist along their length. Unlike the towering growth of other Vallisneria species, this compact cultivar reaches only 8–12 inches (20–30 cm) in height, making it perfect for midground or even large foreground placements in aquascapes.

Native to North America and widely cultivated in Asia, Vallisneria americana 'Asiatica' forms elegant spirals of foliage that create subtle motion in the water column. When planted in dense groups, the twisting leaves add beautiful texture and contrast to an otherwise linear layout. It’s a hardy, beginner-friendly plant that spreads quickly via runners and adapts to a wide range of tank conditions.

🌟 Key Features:

  • Scientific Name: Vallisneria americana 'Asiatica'

  • Synonyms: Twisted Vallisneria, Corkscrew Vallisneria

  • Common Name: Corkscrew Val

  • Twisted Green Leaves: Narrow, spiraled leaves create dynamic visual texture

  • Compact Size: Grows to 8–12 inches, ideal for midground planting

  • Beginner-Friendly: Easy to grow, fast to propagate, and low maintenance

  • Runner Propagation: Quickly forms a thick colony with regular offshoots

🌱 Growth & Propagation:

  • Lighting Needs: Moderate to high light enhances leaf form and color

  • CO₂ & Fertilizers: CO₂ not required; grows best with root tabs or nutrient substrate

  • Growth Method: Spreads via runners; forms dense spiraled clusters over time

  • Nutrient Substrate: Recommended

  • Can Be Grown Emersed: Yes

🖼️ Aquascaping Benefits:

  • Adds movement and texture to midground or foreground zones

  • Provides contrast against broadleaf or red-stemmed species

  • Perfect for softening the transition between low and tall background plants

📦 What You’ll Receive:

Sold as loose fresh-cut plants, bunched plants, or potted plants:

  • Bare-root Plants: Approximately 2–3 individual loose, bare-root plants

  • Bunched Plants: Approximately 2–3 individual bare-root plants bundled with foam and a plant weight

  • Potted Plants: Approximately 2–3 individual bare-root plants potted in rockwool and net pot

⚠️ Disclaimer:

Leaf twist intensity and coloration may vary slightly depending on lighting and nutrients. Some initial melting is normal upon introduction. Stable conditions promote quick propagation and healthy regrowth.

products.title: Mayaca Fluviatilis
products.body_html:
Mayaca fluviatilis – Stream Bogmoss

Mayaca fluviatilis, also known as Stream Bogmoss, is a delicate, fast-growing stem plant treasured for its soft, feathery appearance and vibrant green coloration. Native to the slow-moving waters of Central and South America, this aquatic beauty brings a natural, flowing texture to freshwater aquariums. Its thin, grass-like leaves grow densely along slender stems, creating an elegant, mossy effect that adds depth and movement to any aquascape.

Key Features:

  • Feathery, Flowing Texture: Thin, needle-like leaves grow in dense whorls along the stem, mimicking the look of soft moss or grass.

  • Vibrant Green Coloration: Stays lush green in moderate light but becomes even brighter with high lighting and CO₂.

  • Rapid Growth: Fast-growing under ideal conditions, making it great for filling space or creating lush plant backdrops.

  • Versatile Placement: Suitable for midground to background in planted aquariums, or as a soft accent in nature-style layouts.

Optimal Growing Conditions:

  • Lighting: Performs well under moderate to high light. Stronger lighting encourages more compact, vibrant growth.

  • CO₂ & Nutrients: CO₂ supplementation and regular fertilization will enhance color and speed up growth, but it can adapt to low-tech setups.

  • Water Parameters: pH 5.5–7.5 | Temperature 72–82°F (22–28°C)

Aquarium Benefits:

  • Natural Look: Creates a soft, natural aesthetic that complements other fine-leaved plants and hardscape elements.

  • Shelter for Aquatic Life: Offers hiding spaces for shrimp, fry, and small fish due to its bushy, dense growth.

  • Water Quality Support: Absorbs excess nutrients and helps compete with algae in well-maintained aquariums.

Care & Propagation Tips:

  • Trimming: Regular trimming is recommended to prevent the lower stems from becoming sparse and to encourage bushier growth.

  • Propagation: Propagates easily—cut stems and replant directly into the substrate.

  • Tank Mates: Compatible with a wide range of freshwater fish and invertebrates, including tetras, rasboras, and shrimp.

Conclusion:

Mayaca fluviatilis is an ideal plant for aquarists seeking a soft, flowing texture with rich green tones. Whether you’re designing a jungle-style aquascape or looking to add movement to your midground, this fast-growing stem plant provides beauty, versatility, and functionality. A favorite among hobbyists for its adaptability and graceful form.

Enhance your aquarium with our versatile selection of high-quality aquatic plants, available in three convenient formats:

  • Fresh-Cut Plants: Includes approximately 6+ loose, fresh-cut stems without roots. Perfect for flexible planting arrangements.
  • Fresh-Cut Bunched: Approximately 6+ fresh-cut stems without roots, bundled with foam and a plant weight for easy placement.
  • Fresh-Cut Potted: About 6+ fresh-cut stems without roots, securely potted in a net pot with rockwool, ensuring stability and healthy growth.

Choose the format that best suits your aquascaping needs and create a vibrant, lush underwater environment with our premium aquatic plants.
