products.title: Alternanthera Ficoidea Rainbow
products.body_html:
Alternanthera Ficoidea 'Rainbow' is a vibrant terrarium, vivarium, or bog plant that is sometimes used as an aquatic plant decoration, despite not being a true aquatic species. This plant can be submerged for several months under the right conditions, but it requires periodic emersed growth to restore its energy and continue thriving.

Key Features:

  • Colorful Appearance: Features a stunning array of colors, including shades of red, yellow, and green, adding a rainbow-like effect to any setup.
  • Versatile Habitat: Can be used in terrariums, vivariums, bog gardens, and temporarily in aquariums.

Optimal Conditions:

  • Submerged Growth: Can survive submerged in aquariums for several months. After this period, it should be transitioned to emersed growth.
  • Emersed Growth: Requires high humidity and consistently wet soil when grown above water, especially during recovery phases.
  • Lighting: Thrives in moderate to high lighting conditions, which helps maintain its vibrant color palette.

Aquarium Benefits:

  • Temporary Aquatic Decoration: Provides an attractive and colorful addition to aquariums for several months, developing roots and sometimes growing a few inches taller during its submerged period.
  • Alternative to Aquatic Plants: Its ability to survive underwater for extended periods makes it a popular alternative for hobbyists seeking unique and colorful decorations.

Care and Maintenance:

  • Periodic Transition: To ensure longevity, alternate the plant between submerged and emersed conditions every few months.
  • High Humidity: When grown emersed, maintain consistently high humidity and keep the soil wet to support recovery and growth.

Conclusion:

Alternanthera Ficoidea 'Rainbow' is a versatile and visually stunning plant that can enhance the beauty of terrariums, vivariums, bog gardens, and aquariums. Its ability to survive underwater for extended periods makes it a unique choice for aquarists looking to add a splash of color to their setups. By alternating between submerged and emersed growth, you can enjoy the vibrant presence of Alternanthera Ficoidea 'Rainbow' in various environments.

Enhance your terrarium, vivarium, or aquarium with the stunning and versatile Alternanthera Ficoidea 'Rainbow', and enjoy its vibrant colors and adaptability.

products.title: Guppy Grass
products.body_html:
Najas guadalupensis, commonly known as Guppy Grass, is a popular aquatic plant species highly regarded for its versatility and aesthetic appeal. This submerged plant is native to North and Central America, specifically found in regions such as Texas, Mexico, and the Caribbean.

Key Features:

  • Versatile Growth: Thrives in a variety of aquatic setups, making it suitable for both beginners and experienced aquarists.
  • Aesthetic Appeal: Features long, slender stems with whorls of bright green leaves, providing a natural and attractive look to any aquarium.

Benefits:

  • Rapid Propagation: Known for its fast growth rate, Guppy Grass can quickly create lush, densely planted aquariums.
  • Habitat Creation: Provides a safe and comfortable habitat for fish, fry, and other small aquatic organisms, making it a favorite among breeders.
  • Water Purification: Acts as a natural water purifier by absorbing excess nutrients, helping to prevent algae growth and maintain water quality.
  • Shelter and Protection: Dense vegetation offers shelter for shy or vulnerable fish, contributing to their overall well-being.

Care and Maintenance:

  • Water Parameters: Thrives in a wide range of conditions but prefers slightly alkaline to neutral pH levels (6.5 to 7.5).
  • Lighting: Moderate to high lighting enhances growth and vibrancy.
  • Nutrient Requirements: Benefits from a nutrient-rich substrate or liquid fertilizers.
  • Trimming: Regular trimming is necessary to manage rapid growth and maintain the desired appearance.
  • Propagation: Easily propagated through side shoots, allowing for easy expansion or sharing with fellow aquarists.

Conclusion:

Najas guadalupensis (Guppy Grass) is an excellent choice for those looking to add a versatile, visually appealing, and beneficial plant to their aquarium. Its rapid growth, ease of care, and ability to enhance the overall health of the aquatic environment make it a favored selection among aquarists. Rotala Nanjenshan is a captivating aquatic plant that promises to bring dynamic color and texture to any freshwater aquarium. Known for its delicate, fine-leaved appearance, this plant is a favorite among aquascapers for its ability to create a dense, bushy backdrop or a vibrant midground cluster.

Rotala Nanjenshan is not just beautiful but also functional, helping to oxygenate the water and provide shelter for smaller fish and invertebrates.

Whether you're aiming to create a dramatic 'Dutch style' aquarium or simply enhance your existing setup, Rotala Nanjenshan offers both aesthetic beauty and environmental benefits. It’s an excellent choice for anyone looking to elevate their aquatic garden with vibrant color and intricate textures.

products.title: Rotala Wallichii
products.body_html:
🌸 Rotala wallichii – Fine-Leaf Red Stem Plant

Rotala wallichii is one of the most iconic and visually captivating red stem plants in the aquarium hobby. Known for its needle-shaped leaves and striking coloration that ranges from green to vibrant shades of pink and red, this plant creates an elegant, bushy effect when grown in groups. Though more challenging to maintain than other stem plants, it remains a favorite among aquascapers for its delicate beauty and dramatic color contrast.

🌟 Key Features

  • Scientific Name: Rotala wallichii

  • Leaf Shape: Fine, needle-like leaves with bushy growth pattern

  • Coloration: Green to deep pink and red under high light with CO₂ and iron dosing

  • Growth Form: Upright, fast-growing stem plant

  • Popularity: One of the most admired and commonly used red plants in aquascaping

🌱 Growth & Propagation

  • Lighting Needs: High light required for optimal coloration

  • CO₂ & Fertilizers: Strongly recommended; iron-rich dosing enhances red tones

  • Growth Method: Best planted in groups as midground or background feature

  • Nutrient Substrate: Required for healthy and vigorous growth

  • Can Be Grown Emersed: No, best grown submerged

🖼️ Aquascaping Benefits

  • Color Accent: Adds vibrant pink and red hues, providing contrast against green plants

  • Delicate Texture: Fine, feathery leaves soften aquascapes and add natural depth

  • Focal Grouping: Looks best when planted densely to create a bushy, dramatic effect

products.title: Cameroon Moss
products.body_html:
Cameroon Moss (Plagiochilaceae sp. Cameroon): The Perfect Addition for Freshwater Aquariums

Cameroon Moss, also known as Plagiochilaceae sp. Cameroon, is a popular aquatic moss originating from Cameroon, West Africa. Highly valued for its fast growth and ease of maintenance, this moss is an excellent choice for aquascaping and natural filtration in freshwater aquariums.

Key Features:

  • Rapid Growth: Cameroon Moss grows quickly, helping to maintain a healthy and vibrant aquarium environment.
  • Nutrient Absorption: It efficiently absorbs excess nutrients, enhancing water quality and reducing algae growth.
  • Versatile Use: This moss forms a dense, cushion-like green mat that can be attached to rocks, driftwood, or backgrounds. It also serves as a spawning mop for certain fish species.

Care Tips:

  • Lighting: Provide moderate to low lighting to support healthy growth.
  • Water Changes: Regular water changes are essential to maintain optimal water conditions.
  • Avoid Chemicals: Steer clear of chemical fertilizers or products that can harm the moss and negatively impact water quality.

Benefits:

Cameroon Moss not only adds a natural and aesthetic touch to your aquarium but also creates a habitat for aquatic inhabitants. Its low maintenance requirements make it an ideal choice for both beginner and experienced aquarium enthusiasts.

Enhance your freshwater aquarium with the beauty and functionality of Cameroon Moss. Its ability to improve water quality and provide a natural environment for your fish makes it a must-have for any aquascape.

products.title: Mini Christmas Moss
products.body_html:
Vesicularia montagnei 'Mini', commonly known as Mini Christmas Moss or Mini Xmas Moss, is a beloved aquatic plant among aquarists for its unique, festive appearance and versatile use in aquascaping.

Key Features:

  • Festive Appearance: Features finely branched fronds that resemble miniature Christmas trees, adding a unique and charming element to any aquarium.
  • Compact Growth: Grows densely and compactly, creating a lush, bushy effect that enhances the visual appeal of your aquascape.

Optimal Conditions:

  • Lighting: Thrives in low to moderate lighting conditions, making it suitable for a variety of aquarium setups.
  • Water Parameters: Prefers slightly acidic to neutral pH levels (6.0 to 7.5) and temperatures between 68°F to 77°F (20°C to 25°C).

Aquarium Benefits:

  • Aesthetic Appeal: Adds a lush, green carpet effect to the foreground or midground of your aquarium, enhancing its overall beauty.
  • Versatile Placement: Can be attached to rocks, driftwood, or other decorations, offering flexibility in aquascaping designs.
  • Natural Shelter: Provides excellent hiding and breeding spots for small fish and invertebrates, contributing to a healthy and balanced aquarium ecosystem.

Care and Maintenance:

  • Easy to Care For: Known for its hardiness and minimal care requirements, making it ideal for both beginner and experienced aquarists.
  • Propagation: Can be easily propagated by trimming and reattaching cuttings to new surfaces, allowing for easy expansion and customization of aquascapes.
  • Regular Trimming: Regular trimming helps maintain its compact form and encourages healthy growth.

Conclusion:

Vesicularia montagnei 'Mini' (Mini Christmas Moss) is an exceptional choice for aquarists looking to add a touch of natural elegance to their freshwater aquariums. Its compact, bushy growth and unique miniature Christmas tree appearance make it a standout addition to any aquascape. Easy to care for and highly versatile, Mini Christmas Moss is perfect for creating lush, green carpets or accentuating hardscape elements in your aquarium.

Enhance your aquascape with the charming beauty of Mini Christmas Moss and enjoy the vibrant, healthy underwater environment it helps create.

products.title: Flame Moss
products.body_html:
Taxiphyllum sp. Flame (Flame Moss) is a unique and captivating moss that adds an artistic touch to any freshwater aquarium. Renowned for its flame-like growth pattern, where the fronds grow upward in twisting, wavy shapes, this moss creates an eye-catching vertical effect that enhances the aquascape design.

Key Features:

  • Distinctive Growth Pattern: Flame Moss grows vertically with curling fronds resembling dancing flames, offering a stunning visual texture and movement within the tank.
  • Versatile Aquascaping: Perfect for attaching to driftwood, rocks, or any other aquarium decor, making it an excellent choice for aquascaping enthusiasts looking to add a touch of natural elegance.

Optimal Conditions:

  • Lighting: Thrives under low to moderate lighting conditions, making it suitable for both beginner and advanced aquarists.
  • Water Parameters: Flame Moss prefers temperatures between 68°F to 77°F (20°C to 25°C) and a pH range of 5.0 to 7.5, adapting well to a variety of freshwater setups.

Aquarium Benefits:

  • Natural Shelter & Breeding Ground: Provides excellent cover for shrimp, fry, and other small aquatic creatures, serving as a safe haven and breeding ground.
  • Easy Maintenance: Requires minimal care, making it a hassle-free option for those looking to add greenery to their aquariums without extensive upkeep.
  • Nutrient Absorption: Helps improve water quality by absorbing excess nutrients, contributing to a balanced aquatic environment.

Propagation and Care:

  • Simple Propagation: Can be easily propagated by dividing and attaching small sections to new surfaces in the tank.
  • Low Maintenance: Does not require CO2 supplementation and thrives in various conditions, making it a hardy and adaptable moss for all aquascapers.

Conclusion:

Taxiphyllum sp. Flame (Flame Moss) is an ideal moss for aquascaping enthusiasts seeking to add a dynamic and striking vertical element to their aquarium. Its unique flame-like growth pattern, easy care requirements, and benefits to aquatic life make it a popular choice for enhancing both the beauty and health of freshwater aquariums. Add Flame Moss to your aquascape for a visually stunning and low-maintenance addition that will captivate and charm.

products.title: Weeping Moss Vitro Culture
products.body_html:
Weeping Moss (Vesicularia ferriei) is a popular type of aquarium moss known for its unique, creep-down growing pattern. Native to wet rocks, riverbanks, and moist soils along streams, Weeping Moss adds a lush, cascading effect to any aquascape.

Key Features:

  • Growth Habit: This moss grows downward, creating a beautiful weeping effect that enhances the visual appeal of your aquarium.
  • Attachment: Since Weeping Moss cannot root in the substrate, it must be attached to driftwood, rocks, or other objects. It uses rhizoids, not true roots, to naturally attach to surfaces.

Aquarium Benefits:

  • Nutrient Absorption: Weeping Moss absorbs nutrients from the water column, making it easy to feed and maintain.
  • Habitat Enhancement: Provides excellent hiding and breeding spots for fish and shrimp, contributing to a healthy and diverse aquarium ecosystem.

Care Instructions:

  • Attachment: Secure Weeping Moss to driftwood or rocks using fishing line or cotton thread until it naturally adheres.
  • Environment: It thrives in wet, moist conditions similar to its native habitat, ensuring robust and healthy growth.

Conclusion: Weeping Moss (Vesicularia ferriei) is an exceptional choice for aquarists looking to create a stunning, natural aquascape. Its unique weeping growth pattern, easy attachment, and nutrient absorption from the water column make it a favorite for enhancing the beauty and functionality of freshwater aquariums. Add Weeping Moss to your tank to enjoy its lush, cascading greenery and the habitat it provides for your aquatic inhabitants.

products.title: Bucephalandra Round Wavy
products.body_html:
Bucephalandra Round Wavy (Bucep / Buce plant) is a genus of flowering plants in the family Araceae. There are more than 30 species of Bucephalandra that have been discovered mostly by S.Y. Wong and P.C. Boyce. Most of the species are found in Borneo. Bucephalandra is usually found growing as dense mats over stones or rocks in streams or rivers in moist tropical forests. The colors of the leaf range from dark blue to green, often with white, yellow, red tinges or spots on the back of the leaves. Bucephalandra grows best when attached to the stone, rock, or driftwood.
  • Sold as a Bunched Plants or Potted Plants.

products.title: Bucephalandra Giant Broad Leaf
products.body_html:
🌿 Bucephalandra ‘Giant Broad Leaf’ – Rare Large-Leaf Aquatic Jewel

Bucephalandra ‘Giant Broad Leaf’, commonly known as Buce Giant Broad Leaf, is a striking and rare Bucephalandra variety admired for its wide, paddle-shaped leaves and commanding presence in aquascapes. Originating from the clean, fast-flowing rivers of Borneo, this slow-growing species stands out for its thick, glossy foliage that shimmers in deep green with subtle bluish tones under aquarium lighting. Its large, dramatic leaves bring instant depth and natural elegance to any aquascape—making it a prized gem among collectors and enthusiasts.

🌟 Key Features

  • Scientific Name: Bucephalandra sp. ‘Giant Broad Leaf’

  • Common Name: Buce Giant Broad Leaf

  • Large, Glossy Foliage: Broad, thick leaves with a smooth surface and metallic green-blue iridescence under aquarium light.

  • Hardy & Adaptable: Thrives under low to medium lighting and tolerates a wide range of aquarium conditions.

  • Easy Attachment: Naturally grows on hard surfaces—perfect for driftwood, stones, or decorative hardscape.

  • Aquascaping Centerpiece: Its impressive leaf size makes it a perfect focal point for both nano and large aquariums.

🌱 Growth & Propagation

  • Lighting Needs: Low to medium lighting (higher intensity enhances the iridescent sheen).

  • CO₂ & Fertilizers: Not required but beneficial for faster growth and deeper coloration.

  • Growth Method: Rhizome-based; do not bury the rhizome in substrate—attach to hardscape instead.

  • Nutrient Substrate: Not required; Bucephalandra absorb nutrients directly from the water column.

  • Can Be Grown Emersed: Yes – excellent for terrarium and paludarium setups.

🖼️ Aquascaping Benefits

  • Adds mature structure and contrast to any aquascape.

  • Ideal for midground or background placement or as a showpiece plant on driftwood or rock.

  • Pairs beautifully with mosses, Anubias, or red stem plants for dynamic layouts.

  • Highly durable and easy to maintain—great for both beginners and experts.

📦 What You’ll Receive

Sold as loose bare-root plants:

  • 1 Plant: Loose bare-root with approximately 6+ leaves

  • 5 Plants: Loose bare-root with approximately 25+ leaves total

  • 10 Plants: Loose bare-root with approximately 50+ leaves total

⚠️ Disclaimer

Like most Bucephalandra, slight leaf melt may occur after introduction as the plant adjusts to new conditions. This is temporary—new submersed leaves will regrow from the rhizome once the plant stabilizes.

🚫 State Restrictions Notice ♻️ Be a Responsible Aquatic Plants Owner

Certain aquatic plants may be restricted or regulated in some U.S. states. Always check your local and state regulations before purchasing or introducing plants into natural waterways. Never release aquarium plants into the wild.

products.title: Bucephalandra Theia Green
products.body_html:
Bucephalandra Theia Green is a captivating and rare aquatic plant known for its striking green leaves and unique texture. This plant is highly sought after by aquarists for its beauty and versatility, making it a prized addition to any freshwater aquarium. Bucephalandra is a rheophyte/epiphyte plant, naturally growing over rocks and wood in fast-flowing waters. It takes a very long time to develop into a thick, compact carpet in nature.

Key Features:

  • Striking Green Leaves: Bucephalandra Theia Green boasts vibrant green leaves with a unique, wavy texture. Its lush foliage adds a touch of elegance and a splash of color to any aquascape.

  • Compact Growth: This plant grows in a compact, bushy form, making it ideal for small tanks or as a foreground and midground plant in larger aquariums.

Optimal Conditions:

  • Versatile Attachment: Bucephalandra Theia Green can be easily attached to driftwood, rocks, and other aquarium decorations using fishing line or thread until it establishes its roots. This versatility allows for creative and diverse aquascaping designs.

  • Lighting and Water Parameters: Thrives in low to moderate lighting conditions. It prefers temperatures between 72°F to 82°F (22°C to 28°C) and slightly acidic to neutral pH levels (6.0 to 7.5).

Aquarium Benefits:

  • Aesthetic Enhancement: The vibrant green color and textured leaves of Bucephalandra Theia Green enhance the visual appeal of any aquarium, creating a lush and dynamic underwater landscape.

  • Habitat Creation: This plant provides excellent hiding spots and breeding grounds for small fish and invertebrates, contributing to a balanced and healthy aquarium ecosystem.

Care and Maintenance:

  • Minimal Care Requirements: Bucephalandra Theia Green is relatively easy to care for, requiring only occasional trimming and basic water parameter monitoring.

  • Nutrient Absorption: While it benefits from a nutrient-rich water column, this plant does not require CO2 supplementation. However, CO2 can enhance its growth and vibrancy.

Commitment to Sustainability:

Due to the popularity of Bucephalandra species, they have been heavily collected from the wild, leading to a risk of extinction. At Aquarium Plants Factory, we are committed to providing farm-raised and tissue culture Bucephalandra species to save forests and protect the natural rivers and rainforests in Indonesia.

Conclusion:

Bucephalandra Theia Green is a stunning and versatile plant that brings a touch of natural beauty and elegance to any freshwater aquarium. Its unique foliage and easy care make it a standout addition to any aquascape. Perfect for attaching to driftwood, rocks, and other decorations, Bucephalandra Theia Green is a must-have for aquarists looking to create a lush and vibrant underwater environment.

Transform your aquarium with the exquisite Bucephalandra Theia Green and support sustainable aquascaping practices. Enjoy the serene and captivating beauty it provides.

Enhance your aquarium with our versatile selection of high-quality aquatic plants, available in convenient formats:

  • Rooted Bunched: Approximately 1-2 plants with some roots, bundled with foam and a plant weight for easy placement.
  • Rooted Potted: About 1-2 plants with some roots, securely potted in a net pot with rockwool, ensuring stability and healthy growth.

Choose the format that best suits your aquascaping needs and create a vibrant, lush underwater environment with our premium aquatic plants.

This exotic beauty is ideal for freshwater aquariums, offering a refined texture and color variation that enhances any aquascape layout. With its slow growth and low maintenance needs, Bucephalandra Lalina is perfect for aquarists of all experience levels—from beginners to seasoned aquascaping experts.

Key Features & Benefits

  • Rare Aquatic Plant: Unique rippled foliage with dark green to bluish tones and an iridescent shimmer under aquarium lighting.

  • Low Light Tolerant: Thrives in low to medium lighting, making it suitable for a wide range of aquarium setups.

  • Attach to Hardscape: Best when attached to driftwood, rocks, or aquarium decor—no need for substrate planting.

Simply attach the rhizome to driftwood or rock, and watch this remarkable plant add elegance and depth to your aquascape without demanding constant attention.

Bucephalandra Brownie Ghost is an extraordinary and rare aquatic plant that captivates with its deep, dark hues and unique foliage. This plant is a favorite among aquarists looking to add a touch of mystery and elegance to their aquascapes. Key Features of Bucephalandra Brownie Ghost

  1. Enchanting Dark Foliage: Bucephalandra Brownie Ghost features dark, almost black leaves with subtle iridescent highlights. These leaves create a striking contrast in your aquarium, making this plant a standout feature in any aquascape.

  2. Compact and Bushy Growth: This plant has a compact and bushy growth habit, making it ideal for various aquascaping styles. It can be used as a midground or foreground plant, providing depth and texture to your aquascape.

  4. Natural Habitat: Native to the fast-flowing streams and rivers of Borneo, this plant grows attached to rocks and submerged wood. Its natural habitat makes it an excellent choice for replicating natural biotope aquariums.

  5. Easy Propagation: This plant is easily propagated through rhizome division. Simply cut a section of the rhizome with leaves and roots, and reattach it to a new location in your aquarium. Each section will quickly establish itself and continue to grow.

Care Tips for Bucephalandra Brownie Ghost

  • Lighting: Low to moderate lighting is ideal for Bucephalandra Brownie Ghost. While it can tolerate higher light levels, too much light can promote algae growth on its leaves.

  • Substrate: Bucephalandra Brownie Ghost often does best when attached to rocks or driftwood, mimicking its natural growing conditions. It can also be planted in the substrate if desired.
  • Fertilization: Regular fertilization with a balanced aquarium fertilizer will support healthy growth and vibrant colors. While CO2 supplementation can enhance its development, it is not strictly necessary.
  • Water Flow: A moderate to strong water flow can mimic the plant’s natural environment and help keep its leaves clean and free from debris.

Benefits of Adding Bucephalandra Brownie Ghost to Your Aquarium

  • Aesthetic Appeal: The dark, iridescent foliage of Bucephalandra Brownie Ghost adds a stunning visual effect, enhancing the overall beauty of your aquascape.
  • Habitat Enrichment: Provides natural hiding spots and surfaces for biofilm, benefiting fish and invertebrates.
  • Water Quality Improvement: Like other aquatic plants, Bucephalandra Brownie Ghost helps maintain water quality by absorbing excess nutrients and oxygenating the water.

Bucephalandra Brownie Ghost is an exceptional choice for any aquarist looking to create a vibrant and healthy underwater environment. Its unique appearance and ease of care make it a standout plant for enhancing the beauty and biodiversity of any aquarium.

  2. Compact and Versatile: This plant has a compact growth habit, making it ideal for various aquascaping styles. It can be used as a foreground or midground plant, and its slow growth rate ensures it maintains its shape without frequent trimming.

products.title: Bucephalandra Dark Blue
products.body_html:
Bucephalandra Dark Blue (Bucep / Buce plant) is a genus of flowering plants in the family Araceae. There are more than 30 species of Bucephalandra that have been discovered mostly by S.Y. Wong and P.C. Boyce. Most of the species are found in Borneo. Bucephalandra is usually found growing as dense mats over stones or rocks in streams or rivers in moist tropical forests. The colors of the leaf range from dark blue to green, often with white, yellow, red tinges or spots on the back of the leaves. Bucephalandra grows best when attached to the stone, rock, or driftwood.

  • Sold as a Bunched Plants or Potted Plants.

products.title: Pogostemon Stellatus Dassen Vitro Culture
products.body_html:
Pogostemon stellatus 'Dassen' in Vitro Culture® – Rare, Starburst-Leaf Stem Plant for Advanced Aquascapes

Pogostemon stellatus 'Dassen' is a rare and stunning aquatic stem plant known for its fine, needle-like leaves arranged in a striking starburst pattern. This unique cultivar of Pogostemon stellatus originates from Asia and is prized for its ability to shift in color—ranging from light green to pink, orange, or purple under optimal conditions. Its dynamic structure and intense hues make it an ideal showpiece for Dutch-style aquascapes, color gradients, or high-tech planted tanks.

Each plant is offered in a 4-inch Vitro Culture® bag, containing multiple small, pest-free plantlets grown in vitamin-enriched gel or liquid. Our tissue culture propagation ensures a completely sterile start—100% free of snails, algae, pesticides, and pathogens—making it ideal for shrimp-safe tanks and premium aquascaping setups.

🌿 Key Features:

  • Scientific Name: Pogostemon stellatus 'Dassen'

  • Common Name: Pogostemon 'Dassen'

  • Leaf Shape: Fine, needle-like leaves in starburst whorls

  • Coloration: Green to vivid pink, orange, or purple with strong lighting and nutrients

  • Growth Form: Upright stem plant with dense branching

  • Vitro Culture® Format: 4-inch sealed bag with multiple plantlets

  • Placement: Midground to background

  • Growth Rate: Medium to fast

  • Difficulty Level: Advanced – thrives best in stable, enriched tanks

💧 Optimal Growing Conditions:

  • Lighting: High (essential for vibrant coloration and compact growth)

  • CO₂: Strongly recommended for optimal form and color

  • Substrate: Nutrient-rich substrate or root tab supplementation

  • Water Parameters: pH 6.0–7.2 | Temperature: 22–28°C (72–82°F)

  • Fertilization: Requires regular micronutrient and iron dosing to maintain coloration

  • Trimming: Regular pruning helps promote bushy, compact form

🧪 Vitro Culture® Advantages:

  • 100% Free from Snails, Algae, Pesticides, and Pathogens

  • Clean & Safe: Perfect for shrimp tanks and sensitive ecosystems

  • Long Shelf Life: Can be stored under light until ready to plant

  • Easy to Plant: Rinse, gently divide, and insert into substrate using tweezers

🐠 Aquascaping Benefits:

  • Color Showpiece: Adds a brilliant pop of color and texture to your layout

  • Fine Texture: Balances well with broad-leaf plants and mosses

  • Dutch Layout Star: A must-have for advanced aquascapers building color tiers

  • High Impact: Makes an instant impression in feature spots or mass plantings

📦 What You’ll Receive:

One 4-inch sealed Vitro Culture® bag containing multiple Pogostemon stellatus 'Dassen' plantlets, ready for acclimation and planting.

⚠️ Disclaimer:

Photos are for reference. Actual coloration depends on lighting, CO₂, and nutrient availability. Some melt may occur during the transition to submerged growth, which is normal.

products.title: Limnophila Rugosa
products.body_html:
🌿 Limnophila Rugosa – Basil Leaf Limnophila

Limnophila Rugosa, commonly known as Basil-Leaf Limnophila or Limnophila Butterfly, is an elegant and distinctive aquarium plant native to tropical Asia. Unlike most Limnophila species, it features broad, textured leaves resembling basil, adding a unique charm and texture to aquascapes. Its bushy growth habit and vibrant green coloration make it a standout choice for midground or background planting.

🌟 Key Features:

  • Scientific Name: Limnophila Rugosa

  • Common Name: Basil-Leaf Limnophila, Limnophila Butterfly

  • Family: Plantaginaceae

  • Origin: Tropical Asia

  • Leaf Color: Bright green

  • Growth Form: Bushy, dense stem plant

  • Growth Rate: Slow

  • Difficulty Level: Easy

🌱 Growth & Propagation:

  • Lighting Needs: Medium to high

  • CO₂ & Fertilizers: CO₂ is recommended for optimal growth and leaf health

  • Growth Method: Submersed or emersed

  • Nutrient Substrate: Required for best results

  • Can Be Grown Emersed: Yes

  • Water Temperature: 72–82°F (22–28°C)

  • pH Range: 6.0–7.5

  • Propagation: Simple stem cuttings – replant trimmed stems into substrate for new growth

🖼️ Aquascaping Benefits:

  • Midground Density: Adds lush, vibrant green fullness to aquascape midsections

  • Background Accent: Works beautifully as a natural green backdrop

  • Focal Point Texture: Basil-like leaves provide unique visual contrast in planted layouts

📦 What You’ll Receive:

products.title: Bucephalandra Pink Godzilla
products.body_html:
Bucephalandra Pink Godzilla, also known as Bucephalandra Kedagang Pink, is a captivating and rare aquatic plant that brings a burst of color and elegance to any aquarium. Renowned for its vibrant pink hues and unique foliage, this plant is highly sought after by aquarists looking to create a visually stunning and diverse aquascape. Ideal for both beginners and experienced aquarists, Bucephalandra Pink Godzilla is a must-have for any planted tank.

Key Features of Bucephalandra Pink Godzilla

  1. Vibrant Pink Foliage: Bucephalandra Pink Godzilla boasts striking pink leaves with shades that can range from light pink to deep magenta. This unique coloration adds a dramatic and eye-catching element to your aquarium, making it a focal point in any aquascape.

  2. Compact and Versatile Growth: This plant has a compact growth habit, making it perfect for various aquascaping styles. It can be used as a foreground or midground plant, providing depth and texture to your tank.

  3. Hardy and Adaptable: Known for its resilience, Bucephalandra Pink Godzilla thrives in a range of water conditions. It requires minimal maintenance and can adapt to both low-tech and high-tech aquarium setups, making it suitable for all levels of aquarists.

  4. Natural Habitat: Native to the fast-flowing streams and rivers of Borneo, Bucephalandra Pink Godzilla grows attached to rocks and submerged wood. Its natural habitat makes it an excellent choice for creating biotope aquariums that mimic its native environment.

  5. Easy Propagation: This plant is easily propagated through rhizome division. Simply cut a section of the rhizome with leaves and roots, and attach it to a new location in your aquarium. Each section will quickly establish itself and continue to grow.

Care Tips for Bucephalandra Pink Godzilla

  • Lighting: Low to moderate lighting is ideal. While it can tolerate higher light levels, too much light can promote algae growth on its leaves.
  • Water Conditions: This plant prefers water temperatures between 68-78°F (20-26°C) with a pH range of 6.0 to 7.5. It can thrive in both soft and moderately hard water.
  • Substrate: Bucephalandra Pink Godzilla often does best when attached to rocks or driftwood, mimicking its natural growing conditions. It can also be planted in the substrate if desired.
  • Fertilization: Regular fertilization with a balanced aquarium fertilizer supports healthy growth and vibrant colors. CO2 supplementation can enhance its development but is not strictly necessary.
  • Water Flow: Moderate to strong water flow helps mimic the plant’s natural environment, keeping its leaves clean and free from debris.

Benefits of Adding Bucephalandra Pink Godzilla to Your Aquarium

  • Aesthetic Appeal: The vibrant pink leaves of Bucephalandra Pink Godzilla create a stunning visual effect, enhancing the beauty of any aquascape.
  • Habitat Enrichment: Provides natural hiding spots and surfaces for biofilm, benefiting fish and invertebrates.
  • Water Quality Improvement: Like other aquatic plants, Bucephalandra Pink Godzilla helps maintain water quality by absorbing excess nutrients and oxygenating the water.

Bucephalandra Pink Godzilla is an exceptional choice for any aquarist looking to create a vibrant and healthy underwater environment. Its unique appearance and ease of care make it a standout plant for enhancing the beauty and biodiversity of any aquarium.


Sold as Bare-root Plants or Potted Plants:

  • Bare-root Plants: Approximately 1-2 loose plants 
  • Potted Plants: Approximately 1-2 plants potted in rockwool and a net pot for secure growth and easy placement.

products.title: Hygrophila Odora
products.body_html:
🌿 Hygrophila Odora – Narrow-Leaf Green Alternative to Water Wisteria & Hygrophila pinnatifida

Hygrophila odora is a rare and visually striking African stem plant that combines the delicate, finely divided look of  Water Wisteria (Hygrophila difformis) with the bold, architectural appeal of Hygrophila pinnatifida—while maintaining a clean, vibrant green coloration.

The leaf shape closely resembles Water Wisteria but is much narrower and more refined, giving the plant a sharper, more structured appearance. At the same time, its overall form and texture are very similar to Hygrophila pinnatifida, making Hygrophila odora an excellent green alternative for aquascapers who love pinnatifida’s look but prefer to avoid brown or reddish tones.

When grown submersed, Hygrophila odora produces elegant, deeply lobed green leaves on upright stems, making it ideal for midground to background placement. In emersed form, the leaves broaden slightly, develop a soft texture, and release a natural aromatic scent when touched—a rare and unique trait among aquarium plants.

🌟 Key Features

  • Scientific Name: Hygrophila odora
  • Common Name: Hygrophila Afrika
  • Origin: West Africa
  • Growth Rate: Medium
  • Placement: Midground to Background
  • Color: Bright to deep green
  • Leaf Shape: Narrow, finely divided (similar to Water Wisteria, but slimmer)
  • Comparable To: Hygrophila difformis (Water Wisteria), Hygrophila pinnatifida
  • Unique Trait: Aromatic foliage when grown emersed
  • Difficulty: Beginner to Advanced

🌱 Growth & Propagation

  • Lighting: Medium to High
  • CO₂: Recommended for best structure and faster growth
  • Growth Method: Stem plant
  • Nutrient Substrate: Required
  • Can Be Grown Emersed: Yes
  • Propagation: Trim and replant healthy stem cuttings

With adequate nutrients and CO₂, Hygrophila odora grows strong, upright stems with consistent leaf development. It adapts well to submersed aquariums and also performs exceptionally in paludariums and ripariums when grown emersed.

🖼️ Aquascaping Benefits

  • Offers the fine, feathery look of Water Wisteria in a more compact, refined form
  • Delivers the bold structure of Hygrophila pinnatifida while staying green
  • Excellent contrast plant for red species, dark hardscape, and shadowed layouts
  • Perfect for nature-style, jungle, and collector aquascapes

📦 What You’ll Receive

Fresh, healthy Hygrophila odora cultivated and prepared by Aquarium Plants Factory®, available in convenient planting formats:

Loose Bare-Root Plants:
Approximately 1 individual plant with 4+ healthy leaves, carefully cleaned and ready for planting.

Rooted Bunched Plants:
Approximately 1 rooted plant with 4+ leaves, secured with foam and a plant weight for easy placement.

Rooted Potted Plants:
Approximately 1 rooted plant with 4+ leaves, established in a net pot with rockwool for stability and easy planting.

Photos are for reference only. Actual plants may vary in size, shape, and coloration depending on growth stage and environmental conditions. Minor melting or leaf adjustment may occur as the plant adapts to a new aquarium—this is normal for stem plants.

products.body_html:
🌿 Microsorum pteropus ‘Narrow’ – Slender Java Fern with Upright Leaf Structure

Microsorum pteropus 'Narrow', commonly known as Java Fern Narrow, is a refined and elegant variant of the classic Java Fern, easily recognized by its long, narrow, upright leaves. This cultivar is a favorite among aquascapers for its graceful form and vertical growth, making it an excellent choice for midground or background placement in planted aquariums.

At Aquarium Plants Factory, we cultivate Java Fern Narrow in its emersed form, ensuring a hardy plant ready to transition to submerged conditions. Once established, the slender green fronds gently sway in the current, adding subtle motion and height to your aquascape while maintaining a clean, open aesthetic.

Like all Microsorum species, Java Fern Narrow is a rhizome plant that must be attached to driftwood, stones, or other hardscape elements. It should never be buried in substrate, as doing so may lead to rhizome rot. This plant is slow-growing, low-maintenance, and thrives in low- to medium-light environments with no need for CO₂—ideal for beginners or those running low-tech aquariums.

products.title: Ludwigia Senegalensis Guinea Vitro Culture
products.body_html:
Ludwigia Senegalensis Tissue Culture, also known as Ludwigia Guinea, is a freshwater plant species that belongs to the Onagraceae family. This plant is native to West Africa and is commonly found in marshes, swamps, and slow-moving streams.

This plant has slender, slightly curved green leaves that vary in length and typically grow up to 4-6 centimeters long. With optimal conditions, it has a moderate to fast growth rate and can reach a height of up to 30 centimeters.

Ludwigia Senegalensis is a versatile plant that can be grown both submerged and emerged, making it a popular choice for aquariums due to its attractive appearance and ease of care. The plant can be propagated by stem cuttings, which can then be replanted into the substrate.

To thrive in an aquarium, Ludwigia Senegalensis requires moderate to high lighting and nutrient-rich substrate. It is a relatively undemanding plant, but will benefit from the addition of CO2 and fertilizers. When grown emerged, it is recommended to keep the substrate moist to prevent the plant from drying out.

Interestingly, Ludwigia Senegalensis is often mistaken for another plant species, Ludwigia Glandulosa. While both plants have similar physical characteristics, they differ in their origin and natural habitat. Ludwigia Senegalensis is native to West Africa while Ludwigia Glandulosa is native to North America.

Overall, Ludwigia Senegalensis Tissue Culture, or Ludwigia Guinea, is a beautiful and easy-to-care-for plant that is a great addition to any freshwater aquarium.

  • Sold as a Tissue Culture Plants with vitamin liquid/gel
  • Bag size: Approx. 4 inches. Yield many small plants
  • Tissue Culture Technology | 100% Free from Snail, Algae, Pathogen, and Bacteria plus Extended Shelf Life on all in Vitro Plants

products.title: Dwarf Watermelon (Lindernia Rotundifolia Variegated)

products.title: Java Fern
products.body_html:
🌿 Microsorum pteropus – The Classic and Essential Java Fern

Microsorum pteropus, commonly known as Java Fern, is one of the most iconic and widely used aquatic plants in the aquarium hobby. Native to Southeast Asia, this species is prized for its durability, versatility, and timeless beauty. With its long, arching green leaves and exceptional adaptability, Java Fern remains a staple for both beginner and professional aquascapes.

At Aquarium Plants Factory, our Java Fern is grown in its emersed form, allowing for strong rhizome development and healthy foliage prior to transitioning to submerged conditions. Once introduced into your aquarium, it naturally adapts and becomes a lush focal point or background plant, adding height, texture, and movement to your layout.

Java Fern is a true rhizome plant and should always be attached to driftwood, rock, or aquarium décor. Burying the rhizome in substrate may cause rot. It thrives under low to medium lighting, requires no CO₂, and is exceptionally low maintenance—making it ideal for both low-tech and high-tech aquariums.

🌱 Important Growth & Harvest Information (Please Read)

Our Java Fern plants are shipped as loose, individual rhizome sections or leaves and are not connected as a single, bundled rhizome. This is completely normal and expected.

Java Fern is naturally fragile during harvest, and individual leaves often separate from the main rhizome when collected. This does not affect plant health, growth potential, or long-term success.

We intentionally do not tie Java Fern together using black cotton thread or string to make it appear as though the plant is growing as one mass. While commonly done elsewhere, this practice can:

  • Restrict natural growth
  • Damage the rhizome over time
  • Promote rot if tied too tightly

By shipping Java Fern loose and untied, we ensure you receive healthier, stress-free plants that can be properly mounted and allowed to grow naturally in your aquarium.

🌟 Key Features

  • Aquascaping Classic: Long, arching green leaves create a natural and elegant look
  • Beginner-Friendly: Hardy, forgiving, and perfect for low-tech setups
  • Attachable Growth: Best mounted on wood, stone, or décor—never buried
  • Slow-Growing: Requires minimal trimming and maintenance
  • Versatile Placement: Ideal for mid-ground to background aquascapes

📦 What You’ll Receive

Fresh, healthy Java Fern cultivated and prepared by Aquarium Plants Factory®, available in convenient planting formats:

Bare-Root Plants: Loose, individual Java Fern leaves or rhizome sections (not tied), approximately 6 leaves per order.

Rooted Bunched: Individual Java Fern plants gently bundled with foam and a plant weight for secure anchoring, approximately 6 leaves total.

Rooted Potted: Java Fern grown in rockwool and placed in a net pot for easy attachment and stable mounting, approximately 6 leaves per pot.

⚠️ Disclaimer

Photos are sample representations. Due to the natural growth and harvest process, Java Fern may arrive as individual leaves or rhizome pieces rather than a single connected cluster. This is normal and does not impact plant health or future growth.

🚫 State Restrictions Notice ♻️ Be a Responsible Aquatic Plants Owner

Certain aquatic plants may be restricted or regulated in some U.S. states. Always check your local and state regulations before purchasing or introducing plants into natural waterways. Never release aquarium plants into the wild.

🌿 Anubias nana ‘Golden’ on Driftwood – Rare Golden Accent, Ready to Place

Anubias nana ‘Golden’, also known as Anubias Golden Nana, is a rare and highly desirable Anubias variety prized for its lighter yellow-green to golden foliage. Compared to standard Anubias Nana, this cultivar offers a brighter, more vibrant look that creates stunning contrast against dark hardscape and shaded aquariums.

In this listing, each plant comes professionally mounted on natural Malaysian driftwood, making it a ready-to-place aquascaping accent straight out of the box—no tying, gluing, or planting required.

  • Plant Type: Rhizome / Epiphyte

  • Difficulty Level: Beginner

This slow-growing Anubias is extremely hardy and ideal for aquarists seeking a low-maintenance plant with premium visual appeal.

🌱 Growth & Care Information

  • Lighting: Low to moderate (brighter light enhances golden tones)
  • CO₂: Not required
  • Nutrient Substrate: Not required
  • Can Be Grown Emersed: Yes

Important: Anubias is a rhizome plant. The rhizome must remain exposed and should never be buried in substrate. Mounting on driftwood ensures proper airflow and prevents rot.

🪵 Driftwood & Mounting Details

Each set includes a small, boil-treated piece of natural Malaysian driftwood, approximately 3–4 inches in size, carefully selected for aquarium use. Every driftwood piece features a pre-drilled hole to ensure a secure, stable fit.

The Anubias Golden Nana is expertly wrapped in rockwool, providing optimal support while encouraging healthy root development. This method creates a strong foundation without restricting the rhizome, allowing the plant to establish naturally over time.

The driftwood is fully aquarium-safe and prepared to release only minimal tannins, helping maintain clear, balanced water conditions. Each piece is naturally unique in shape and character.

🖼️ Aquascaping Benefits

  • Perfect for nano tanks, shrimp tanks, betta tanks, and larger displays
  • Ideal for placement on driftwood, rocks, or decorative hardscape
  • Safe for fish, shrimp, and snails

products.title: Ammannia Crassicaulis
products.body_html:
🌿 Ammannia Crassicaulis – Bronze-Red Stem Beauty

Ammannia crassicaulis (formerly sold under the name Nesaea) is an elegant stem plant admired for its graceful form and rich, warm coloration. At Aquarium Plants Factory®, we cultivate this species in its emersed form, where the leaves appear green. Once transitioned to submerged growth under intense lighting, CO₂ injection, and nutrient-rich conditions, the plant develops stunning bronze, orange, and red tones, making it a beautiful focal plant for advanced aquascapes.

While similar to Ammannia gracilis, this variety displays a softer, golden-bronze hue instead of bright red. It thrives best when stems are spaced apart to allow proper water movement and light penetration.

🌟 Key Features

• Scientific Name: Ammannia crassicaulis
• Common Name: Red Ammannia
• Family Name: Lythraceae
• Native To: Africa and Madagascar
• Growth Form: Upright stem plant with narrow lanceolate leaves
• Coloration: Green (emersed) → Bronze-Red (submerged, high light)
• Lighting: Intense lighting required for best color
• Requirements: Demanding – requires CO₂ and nutrient-rich substrate
• Growth Rate: Moderately fast under good conditions

🌱 Growth & Propagation

products.title: Java Fern Black Forest
products.body_html:
🌿 Microsorum pteropus ‘Black Forest’ – Deep-Green Java Fern with Dense, Textured Foliage

Microsorum pteropus ‘Black Forest’, commonly known as Java Fern Black Forest, is a visually rich and adaptable aquatic plant originating from the Indonesian island of Java and also found in Malaysia, Thailand, Northeast India, and parts of China. This striking variety is celebrated for its dark green, deeply textured leaves, which form dense, bushy growth, bringing structure and mystery to freshwater aquascapes.

At Aquarium Plants Factory, we grow this plant in its emersed form, where it develops strong, healthy rhizomes that adapt easily to submerged aquarium life. Once underwater, Java Fern Black Forest maintains its dramatic leaf color and distinctive shape—making it a favorite for adding shadowy contrast and visual depth in midground to background placements.

Like all Java Ferns, this variety is a rhizome-based plant and should be attached to driftwood, stones, or hardscape—never buried in substrate. It thrives in low- to medium-light environments, requires no CO₂, and is extremely beginner-friendly, making it an ideal choice for both simple and advanced planted tank designs.

🌟 Key Features:

  • Dark, Forest-Green Foliage: Deep green, textured leaves bring bold contrast to aquascapes

  • Dense and Lush Growth: Fills space effectively with bushy, natural form

  • Attachable Rhizome Plant: Mount to wood, rocks, or decor—no substrate needed

  • Low Maintenance: Slow-growing and ideal for low-tech tanks without CO₂

  • Emersed Grown: Strong and ready to transition to submerged conditions

🔎 Botanical Information:

  • Scientific Name: Microsorum pteropus ‘Black Forest’

  • Synonyms: Leptochilus pteropus ‘Black Forest’

  • Common Names: Java Fern Black Forest

  • Plant Type: Rhizome plant

  • Placement: Attach to driftwood, stone, or hardscape

  • Growth Rate: Slow

  • Lighting: Low to Medium

  • Origin: Southeast Asia (Java, Malaysia, Thailand, India, China)

💡 Optimal Growing Conditions:

  • Lighting: Low to medium light for healthy, slow growth

  • CO₂ & Fertilization: CO₂ not required; occasional fertilization supports vitality

  • Water Parameters:

    • pH: 6.0–7.5

    • Temperature: 68–82°F (20–28°C)

    • Water Hardness: Soft to moderately hard

🌱 Growth & Propagation:

  • CO₂ Requirement: Not required

  • Nutrient Substrate: Not required

  • Can Grow Emersed: Yes

  • Propagation Method: Rhizome division

📦 What You’ll Receive:

Sold as loose bare-root plants, bunched plants, or potted plants:

  • Bare-root Plants: Approximately 6+ leaf portion, loose rhizome

  • Bunched Plants: Approximately 6+ leaf portion bundled with foam and plant weight

  • Potted Plants: Approximately 6+ leaf portion, potted in rockwool and net pot

products.title: Bucephalandra Skeleton Queen
products.body_html:
Bucephalandra Skeleton Queen is a rare and highly sought-after aquatic plant that brings a touch of regal elegance to any aquarium. Known for its distinctive skeletal-like leaf structure and vibrant hues, this plant is a favorite among aquarists looking to create a unique and stunning aquascape. Whether you're a beginner or an experienced aquarist, Bucephalandra Skeleton Queen is an ideal choice for enhancing the beauty and biodiversity of your tank.

Key Features of Bucephalandra Skeleton Queen

  1. Striking Leaf Structure: Bucephalandra Skeleton Queen features uniquely patterned leaves that resemble a skeleton, with vibrant colors ranging from deep green to shades of blue and purple. This intricate leaf structure adds a dramatic and eye-catching element to any aquascape.

  2. Compact and Versatile: This plant has a compact growth habit, making it perfect for various aquascaping styles. It can be used as a foreground or midground plant, providing depth and texture to your aquarium.

  3. Hardy and Adaptable: Bucephalandra Skeleton Queen is known for its resilience and adaptability. It thrives in a range of water conditions and requires minimal maintenance, making it suitable for both low-tech and high-tech aquarium setups.

  4. Natural Habitat: Native to the fast-flowing streams and rivers of Borneo, Bucephalandra Skeleton Queen grows attached to rocks and submerged wood. Its natural growth habit makes it an excellent choice for creating biotope aquariums that mimic its natural environment.

  5. Easy Propagation: This plant is easily propagated through rhizome division. Simply cut a section of the rhizome with leaves and roots, and attach it to a new location in your aquarium. Each section will quickly establish itself and continue to grow.

Care Tips for Bucephalandra Skeleton Queen

  • Lighting: Low to moderate lighting is ideal for Bucephalandra Skeleton Queen. While it can tolerate higher light levels, too much light can promote algae growth on its leaves.
  • Water Conditions: This plant prefers water temperatures between 68-78°F (20-26°C) with a pH range of 6.0 to 7.5. It can thrive in both soft and moderately hard water.
  • Substrate: Bucephalandra Skeleton Queen often does best when attached to rocks or driftwood, mimicking its natural growing conditions. It can also be planted in the substrate if desired.
  • Fertilization: Regular fertilization with a balanced aquarium fertilizer supports healthy growth and vibrant colors. CO2 supplementation can enhance its development but is not strictly necessary.
  • Water Flow: Moderate to strong water flow helps mimic the plant’s natural environment, keeping its leaves clean and free from debris.

Benefits of Adding Bucephalandra Skeleton Queen to Your Aquarium

  • Aesthetic Appeal: The unique skeletal-like leaves of Bucephalandra Skeleton Queen create a stunning visual effect, enhancing the beauty of any aquascape.
  • Habitat Enrichment: Provides natural hiding spots and surfaces for biofilm, benefiting fish and invertebrates.
  • Water Quality Improvement: Like other aquatic plants, Bucephalandra Skeleton Queen helps maintain water quality by absorbing excess nutrients and oxygenating the water.

Bucephalandra Skeleton Queen is an exceptional choice for any aquarist looking to create a vibrant and healthy underwater environment. Its unique appearance and ease of care make it a standout plant for enhancing the beauty and biodiversity of any aquarium.

products.title: Cryptocoryne Lucens Vitro Culture

Cryptocoryne x willisii 'Lucens' commonly known as Cryptocoryne Lucens, is a versatile and resilient aquarium plant. Ideal for both beginners and experienced aquarists, it adds a touch of natural beauty to any tank.

Cryptocoryne Lucens features slender, lance-shaped leaves ranging from light to dark green. Its compact, rosette growth pattern creates a lush, dense carpet, perfect for the foreground or midground of your aquarium.

Care Requirements

Propagates through runners. Allow the plant to spread naturally or separate and replant the runners to encourage new growth.

Benefits and Placement

  • Aesthetic Appeal: Adds a lush, green appearance to your aquarium.
  • Versatility: Ideal for foreground and midground placement.
  • Low Maintenance: Easy to care for, making it suitable for all levels of aquarists.

Conclusion

Enhance your aquarium with Cryptocoryne Lucens. Its attractive appearance, ease of care, and adaptability make it a must-have for any aquascape. Order today and transform your tank into a vibrant, green paradise.

products.title: Helanthium Quadricostatus (Echinodorus) Vitro Culture
products.body_html:
Helanthium bolivianum 'Quadricostatus', also known as Echinodorus bolivianus 'Quadricostatus', is a popular aquatic plant among aquarium enthusiasts. It is a species of the Echinodorus genus and is native to Bolivia, Brazil, and Peru.

The plant is characterized by its long and narrow leaves that grow up to 20 inches in height and have a distinct reddish-brown coloration. Its leaves are arranged in a rosette pattern, and it forms runners that allow it to propagate and spread.

Helanthium bolivianum 'Quadricostatus' is a hardy plant that can thrive in a wide range of water conditions. It prefers moderate to high lighting and a nutrient-rich substrate. It can also tolerate a wide range of water hardness and pH levels.

In aquariums, Helanthium bolivianum 'Quadricostatus' is often used as a foreground or mid-ground plant, where its striking coloration and dense foliage can create a lush and vibrant underwater landscape. It also provides a natural habitat for small fish and invertebrates to hide and forage.

products.title: Schismatoglottis Prietoi Vitro Culture
products.body_html:
Schismatoglottis Prietoi, an aquatic plant native to the Philippines, is a unique addition to the aquascaping world. It belongs to the family Araceae and is notable for its ability to thrive in fully aquatic environments, a rare trait within its genus. This small, colonial plant grows only up to 8 cm tall, featuring pale green to green, oblong to elliptical leaves with a smooth texture, sharply pointed tips, and broadly wavy edges.

Ideal for aquariums, Schismatoglottis Prietoi is known for its ease of care and adaptability to various water conditions. It prefers medium to low lighting and can flourish in temperatures ranging from 20 to 28°C. The plant's pH tolerance spans from 6.5 to 7.5, accommodating both soft and hard water types. While CO2 injection can promote faster growth, it's not a necessity for this plant.

Schismatoglottis Prietoi is particularly suited for smaller aquarium setups like Nano Cubes due to its compact growth pattern. It can be planted directly into the substrate or attached to hardscape materials like rocks and driftwood. This versatility, combined with its unique appearance and easy propagation through rhizomes, makes Schismatoglottis Prietoi a valuable plant for both beginner and experienced aquarists.

This species is a testament to the diversity and beauty of aquatic plants, offering a distinct look compared to more common aquarium plants like Anubias. It's a perfect choice for aquarists looking to create a natural, riverbed-like environment in their tanks.

products.title: Eriocaulon Cinereum

Eriocaulon cinereum, commonly known as Erio Cinereum, is a captivating aquatic plant celebrated for its star-shaped rosette growth and resemblance to a miniature sea urchin. This compact foreground species adds a bold, architectural element to nature-style aquascapes and is especially valued for its symmetry and clean form.

When provided with soft water, bright lighting, and CO₂, Eriocaulon cinereum flourishes—sometimes producing slender flowering stalks that enhance its ornamental beauty. Grown under controlled lab conditions, this plant arrives healthy and pest-free, perfect for sensitive tanks and advanced aquascaping projects.

  • Color: Deep green to bluish-green

  • Temperature Range: 68°F to 78°F (20°C to 26°C)

products.body_html:

Limnophila sp. ‘Belem’ is a rare and elegant aquarium plant admired for its delicate textures and compact growth. Originally discovered near Belem, Brazil, this stem plant is distinguished by its long, narrow, serrated leaves that are naturally green but can shift into vibrant shades of red to deep purple depending on lighting intensity, CO₂ supplementation, and nutrient availability. Its fine-leafed structure makes it an excellent choice for adding contrast, color variety, and depth to aquascapes.

🌟 Key Features:

  • Scientific Name: Limnophila sp. ‘Belem’

  • Common Name: Belem Needle Leaf

  • Origin: Brazil (Belem region)

  • Unique Foliage: Long, narrow, serrated leaves with vivid red coloration under high-tech conditions.

  • Compact Growth: Shorter and bushier than most Limnophila species, perfect for aquascaping.

  • Color Potential: Green foliage that transitions to reddish tones with high light and CO₂.

🌱 Optimal Growing Conditions:

products.title: Hydrocotyle ranunculoides ‘Variegated’
products.body_html:
Hydrocotyle ranunculoides ‘Variegated’ – Floating Pennywort

Hydrocotyle ranunculoides ‘Variegated’ is a stunning aquatic plant admired for its clover-like, scalloped leaves with vibrant green centers and creamy-white margins. The distinctive umbrella-shaped foliage floats gracefully on the water’s surface or creeps along edges, adding a fresh, luminous accent to ponds, aquariums, and container water gardens.

The variegated form offers a refined ornamental look, while its larger clover-shaped leaves resemble those of Hydrocotyle sibthorpioides but are noticeably broader and more striking. Whether floating freely or rooted in shallow margins, it instantly adds a natural, elegant touch to any aquascape.

At Aquarium Plants Factory®, each plant is carefully cultivated for optimal health and vibrant variegation, ensuring that it arrives ready to thrive and beautify your setup with its distinctive charm.

🌟 Key Features

  • Scientific Name: Hydrocotyle ranunculoides ‘Variegated’

  • Common Name: Variegated Floating Pennywort, Marble Floating Pennywort

  • Appearance: Round, scalloped, non-peltate leaves with creamy-white edges and green centers

  • Growth Habit: Floating or creeping stems that root at nodes

  • Height: Low-growing, averaging around 6 inches tall

  • Native Range: North America (native aquatic species)

  • USDA Hardiness: Zones 5–11

🌱 Growth & Care

  • Water Depth: Grows best in moist soil or shallow water up to 4 inches deep

  • Water Quality: Prefers clear, clean water for vibrant color and healthy growth

  • Temperature: Hardy across USDA zones 5–11

  • Placement: Excellent for pond margins, floating arrangements, or shallow planters

  • Fertilizer: Use a balanced aquatic fertilizer as needed for optimal growth

🖼️ Display Ideas

  • Complements lilies, lotus, and other marginal aquatics

  • Great choice for decorative bowls, mini ponds, or indoor water features

products.title: Glossostigma Elatinoides
products.body_html:
Glossostigma elatinoides, commonly referred to as 'Glosso' or 'small mud-mat'. It is a popular aquatic plant among aquarium enthusiasts, prized for its vibrant green color and carpeting growth habit. Originating from New Zealand, this delicate plant features small, rounded leaves and spreads quickly, forming a dense, lush carpet along the substrate of freshwater aquariums.

Characteristics:

Glossostigma elatinoides belongs to the Scrophulariaceae family and hails from New Zealand. It boasts bright green leaves and has a fast growth rate, typically reaching a height of 2-3 cm (0.8-1.2 inches). To thrive, it requires high to very high lighting and a substantial amount of CO2.

Care and Maintenance:

To maintain a healthy and thriving Glossostigma elatinoides carpet, it's essential to provide high-intensity lighting and consistent CO2 supplementation. This plant also benefits from a nutrient-rich substrate and regular fertilization. With proper care, Glosso will quickly cover the aquarium floor, providing an attractive, natural look.

Aquascaping Tips:

Glossostigma elatinoides is ideal for foreground placement, creating a seamless, green carpet effect. It is perfect for Dutch and Nature-style aquascapes, adding texture and visual interest. For best results, plant in small clusters, spacing them a few centimeters apart to encourage spreading. Regular trimming is necessary to prevent overgrowth and maintain a neat appearance.

Glossostigma elatinoides is a beautiful and versatile plant, making it a favorite choice for aquarists seeking to create stunning underwater landscapes.

products.title: Bucephalandra Rainbow Marble
products.body_html:
Bucephalandra Rainbow Marble (Bucep / Buce plant) is a genus of flowering plants in the family Araceae. There are more than 30 species of Bucephalandra which have been discovered mostly by S.Y. Wong and P.C. Boyce. Most of the species are found in Borneo. Bucephalandra is usually found growing as dense mats over stones or rocks in streams or rivers in moist tropical forests. The colors of the leaf range from dark blue to green, often with white, yellow, red tinges or spots on the back of the leaves. Bucephalandra grows best when attached to the stone, rock or driftwood. 
  • Sold as a small loose, bare-root rhizome plant with 4+ leaves.

products.title: Rotala Indica (Ammannia Bonsai)
products.body_html:
Rotala Indica 'Bonsai,' also known as the true Rotala Indica, is often confused with Rotala Rotundifolia, a completely different species. Originally introduced as Ammannia sp. 'Bonsai,' its true identity was confirmed by Cavan Allen in 2009.

Distinctive Features: Rotala Indica 'Bonsai' is characterized by its thick, round leaves, with the tips turning a beautiful reddish hue under strong lighting. Unlike Rotala Rotundifolia, which tends to creep under intense light, Rotala Indica 'Bonsai' grows straight and upright, making it a striking addition to any aquascape.

Growth and Care: This plant is highly versatile, thriving in various aquarium settings. It is particularly popular in Street/Dutch-style aquariums and is ideal for small to nano tanks. Rotala Indica 'Bonsai' looks best when planted in small to medium groups, creating a lush and vibrant display.

Aquascaping Uses:

  • Dutch-Style Aquariums: Perfect for creating structured, layered layouts.
  • Nano Tanks: Its compact size makes it an excellent choice for smaller aquariums.
  • Group Planting: Grows beautifully in clusters, adding depth and color to your aquascape.

Enhance your aquarium with the unique beauty and growth habit of Rotala Indica 'Bonsai'. Shop now to add this true Rotala Indica to your collection and elevate your aquascaping designs.

Enhance your aquarium with our versatile selection of high-quality aquatic plants, available in three convenient formats:

  • Fresh-Cut Plants: Includes approximately 6+ loose, fresh-cut stems without roots. Perfect for flexible planting arrangements.
  • Fresh-Cut Bunched: Approximately 6+ fresh-cut stems without roots, bundled with foam and a plant weight for easy placement.

products.title: Utricularia Graminifolia
products.body_html:
Utricularia Graminifolia is celebrated as one of the most beautiful and popular foreground plants for planted aquariums. Its small size, fluffy appearance, and grass-like lawn effect make it a top choice for aquascaping, especially in nano tanks.

Key Features

  • Scientific Name: Utricularia Graminifolia
  • Common Name: UG, Grass-leaved Bladderwort
  • Family: Lentibulariaceae
  • Leaf Color: Bright green
  • Growth Form: Dense, carpet-like

Unique Characteristics

Utricularia Graminifolia stands out for its bright green, grass-like leaves that form a lush, dense carpet, creating a stunning visual effect in the foreground of any aquarium. Its compact size and fluffy texture make it ideal for intricate aquascape designs.

Ideal Conditions

To thrive, Utricularia Graminifolia requires specific conditions:

  • Light: High intensity
  • CO2: Beneficial for optimal growth
  • Substrate: Nutrient-rich soil
  • Water Temperature: 22-28°C (72-82°F)
  • pH Range: 6.0-7.5

Care and Maintenance

This plant is relatively easy to care for, given the right conditions:

  • Lighting: Ensure high light levels to promote healthy growth.
  • Anchoring: Due to its tiny root system, use stainless steel T-pins to anchor the plant to the substrate until it becomes established.
  • Dry Start Method (DSM): The DSM is highly effective for quickly establishing Utricularia Graminifolia, allowing it to access natural CO2 and grow rapidly.

Growth and Propagation

Once adapted to the environment, Utricularia Graminifolia develops a deep root system, spreading through runners to form a continuous carpet. The dry start method (DSM) is particularly effective, as it allows the plant to access an unlimited amount of natural CO2, boosting growth rates and ensuring a fully grown carpet in a short time.

Safety in Aquariums

Despite being a carnivorous plant, Utricularia Graminifolia is safe for aquarium inhabitants. Its tiny traps are designed to capture microscopic organisms and pose no threat to fish, shrimp, or other larger tank inhabitants. This makes it a unique and harmless addition to your aquascape.

Aquascaping Uses

Utricularia Graminifolia is versatile and ideal for various aquascaping applications:

  • Foreground Plant: Perfect for creating a dense, green carpet in the foreground.
  • Nano Tanks: Ideal for small tanks due to its compact and fluffy growth.
  • Carpet Plant: Its grass-like appearance makes it an excellent choice for lush carpeted areas.

The bright green, dense foliage of Utricularia Graminifolia provides excellent contrast and visual interest, enhancing the overall aesthetics of both Dutch and Nature-style aquascapes.

Utricularia Graminifolia is a must-have for any aquarist looking to enhance their aquarium with a beautiful and unique foreground plant. Its vibrant green color, fluffy texture, and easy maintenance make it a standout choice for creating a stunning underwater landscape.

  • Sold as a small loose bare-root portion
  • Approx. 2" x 2" in size

products.title: Bucephalandra Velvet Tricolor
products.body_html:
🌿 Bucephalandra sp. "Velvet Tricolor" – Rare Bushy Epiphyte

Bucephalandra sp. "Velvet Tricolor" (sometimes referred to as Velvet 3 Color) is a rare, small, and densely bushy Bucephalandra form with narrow, wavy leaves. Believed to originate from the Sanggau area in West Kalimantan, Borneo, its precise species classification and exact locality remain uncertain. Despite its rarity, this plant has proven to be highly suitable for aquariums, with excellent growth habits and steady propagation.

Young leaves emerge in shades of light brown-red, later maturing into deep dark brown-green, creating a striking multicolor effect that inspires its name. Its compact, ramified rhizome structure forms a bushy cluster, making it a unique addition to any aquascape.

🌟 Key Features:

  • Scientific Name: Bucephalandra sp. "Velvet Tricolor"

  • Origin: West Kalimantan, Borneo (Indonesia)

  • Growth Form: Small, narrow-leaved Bucephalandra with bushy habit

  • Leaf Shape & Size: Linear, wavy leaves approx. 30 x 4.5 mm, with a 5 mm petiole

  • Coloration: Young leaves light brown-red, mature leaves dark brown-green

  • Height: Reaches around 6 cm when grown submerged

  • Rarity: Rarely available in the hobby, propagated in aquaria for over a year

🌱 Growth & Propagation:

  • Lighting Needs: Medium lighting recommended

  • CO₂ & Fertilization: Benefits from CO₂ injection, good nutrient supply, and steady water current

  • Growth Method: Epiphyte – best attached to rocks or driftwood (avoid burying the rhizome)

  • Nutrient Substrate: Not required

  • Can Be Grown Emersed: Yes, though usually propagated submerged in aquaria

  • Propagation: Easily propagated via rhizome cuttings, forming dense clusters over time

🖼️ Aquascaping Benefits:

  • Perfect as a low-growing bushy epiphyte in aquascapes

  • Offers subtle color contrast with red-brown to green hues

  • Excellent for hardscape decoration on rocks and driftwood

  • Suitable for nano aquascapes as well as larger aquariums due to its slow but steady propagation

📦 What You’ll Receive:

Sold as Bare-root Plants:

  • 1 Plant: Loose bare-root with approximately 6+ leaves

  • 5 Plants: Loose bare-root with approximately 25+ leaves in total

  • 10 Plants: Loose bare-root with approximately 50+ leaves in total

⚠️ Disclaimer:

Like all Bucephalandra species, growth rate may vary depending on tank conditions. Ensure the rhizome is never buried in substrate, as this may cause rot. Stable water parameters and patience are key for long-term success.

🚫 State Restrictions Notice ♻️ Be a Responsible Aquatic Plants Owner

Please check your local regulations regarding the import or keeping of aquatic plants. Never release aquarium plants into local waterways.

Nymphaea micrantha is a beautiful and rare dwarf lotus prized for its unique tri-color foliage, displaying shades of green, bronze, red, and purple depending on lighting and nutrients. Native to tropical West Africa, this compact water lily makes an excellent centerpiece plant for planted aquariums without overwhelming smaller tanks.

With proper care, Nymphaea micrantha produces elegant submerged leaves and may send surface leaves if allowed, creating a natural and dynamic aquascape.

products.title: Anubias Petite White Platinum
products.body_html:
🤍 Anubias Petite White Platinum – Ultra-Rare Albino Anubias for Elite Aquascapes

Anubias Petite White Platinum is an exceptionally rare albino cultivar of Anubias barteri var. nana ‘Petite’, prized for its pure white to platinum foliage, miniature growth, and collector-grade rarity. With almost no chlorophyll, this plant delivers a striking contrast against dark hardscape and green plants, making it a true statement piece for premium aquascapes.

Due to its albino nature and extremely slow growth, this plant is recommended for experienced aquarists only. When placed correctly, it offers unmatched elegance and exclusivity in nano tanks and high-end layouts.

products.title: Rotala Macrandra Vitro Culture

Farm-raised Rotala Macrandra is easily broken in transit because of the fragile stems. It is also harder to adapt to the new environment when the plant reaches the mature stage. products.title: Bolbitis Heudelotii Vitro Culture

Its flowing, feather-like foliage sways gently in the current, providing a tranquil, jungle-like aesthetic to any freshwater tank.

Now available in a compact 2.5-inch Vitro Culture® cup, this plant is grown in vitamin-enriched gel using sterile tissue culture technology. Each cup contains healthy, pest-free plantlets that are 100% free from snails, algae, pesticides, and pathogens, making it ideal for shrimp tanks, nano aquariums, and sensitive livestock environments.

products.title: Ceratopteris Siliquosa Vitro Culture
products.body_html:
Ceratopteris Siliquosa, commonly known as Water Sprite, is a highly versatile and fast-growing aquatic plant that can transform any aquarium into a lush, green oasis. Known for its delicate, fern-like leaves and easy care requirements, Water Sprite is an excellent choice for both beginner and experienced aquarists. This plant can be used as a floating plant, a midground anchor, or as a background plant, offering flexibility in aquascaping designs.

Key Features of Ceratopteris Siliquosa (Water Sprite)

  1. Delicate, Fern-Like Leaves: Water Sprite boasts finely divided, lace-like leaves that create a beautiful and airy appearance. The light green foliage adds a refreshing and natural look to any aquarium, making it an attractive focal point.

  2. Versatile Growth Options: This plant can be grown either floating on the water surface or anchored in the substrate. Its adaptability allows for creative aquascaping, whether you want a floating canopy or a rooted background plant.

  3. Fast Growth Rate: Ceratopteris Siliquosa is known for its rapid growth, helping to quickly fill in empty spaces in your tank. This fast growth rate also makes it effective at outcompeting algae for nutrients, contributing to a cleaner and healthier aquarium environment.

  4. Natural Habitat: Native to tropical regions around the world, Water Sprite thrives in warm, freshwater environments. It naturally grows in shallow waters, making it an ideal choice for replicating tropical biotope aquariums.

  5. Easy Propagation: Propagating Water Sprite is simple and straightforward. New plantlets form along the leaves and can be separated and replanted. This makes it easy to expand your collection and maintain a lush, green aquarium.

Care Tips for Ceratopteris Siliquosa (Water Sprite)

  • Lighting: Moderate to high lighting is ideal for Water Sprite. Adequate light supports its rapid growth and helps maintain its vibrant green color.
  • Water Conditions: This plant prefers water temperatures between 68-82°F (20-28°C) with a pH range of 6.0 to 7.5. It can thrive in a variety of water conditions, making it highly adaptable.
  • Substrate: While Water Sprite can be grown floating, it can also be anchored in the substrate. A nutrient-rich substrate will support its growth, but it can also absorb nutrients directly from the water.
  • Fertilization: Regular fertilization with a balanced aquarium fertilizer will promote healthy growth and vibrant foliage. CO2 supplementation is not strictly necessary but can enhance its development.
  • Water Flow: Moderate water flow is ideal for keeping the leaves of Water Sprite clean and free from debris, mimicking its natural environment.

Benefits of Adding Ceratopteris Siliquosa (Water Sprite) to Your Aquarium

  • Aesthetic Appeal: The delicate, fern-like leaves of Water Sprite add a stunning visual effect, enhancing the natural beauty of any aquascape.
  • Habitat Enrichment: Provides excellent cover and hiding spots for fish and invertebrates, creating a more dynamic and balanced ecosystem.
  • Water Quality Improvement: Water Sprite helps maintain water quality by absorbing excess nutrients and oxygenating the water, reducing the risk of algae outbreaks.

Ceratopteris Siliquosa (Water Sprite) is an exceptional choice for any aquarist looking to create a vibrant and healthy underwater environment. Its unique appearance, fast growth, and ease of care make it a standout plant for enhancing the beauty and biodiversity of any aquarium.

products.title: Bacopa Platinum Vitro Culture
products.body_html:
Bacopa Monnieri Platinum is a unique version of the popular Bacopa Monnieri plant used in aquariums. At Aquarium Plants Factory, we're among the first to offer this special type of plant. The original Bacopa Monnieri, also called Moneywort or Water Hyssop, is easy to look after and grows fast in many settings. But this new version, the Platinum, is not common. It's harder to take care of because it doesn't have the usual green color that helps it photosensitive from sunlight.

You can put Bacopa Monnieri Platinum in the front part of your aquarium, but you need to look after it by trimming and taking care of it. When there's not a lot of light, it might get long and thin as it reaches for the light. But if you give it strong light and good nutrients, it will stay short and the stems will spread out really close to the bottom of the tank, like a creeping plant. Just remember, this special plant needs a bit more attention than the regular green one.

products.title: Java Fern Large Size
products.body_html:
🌿 Microsorum pteropus – Large Size Java Fern for Instant Impact

Microsorum pteropus, commonly known as Java Fern, is one of the most beloved and beginner-friendly aquatic plants in the hobby. This special large size variant, standing approximately 8–10 inches tall, is ideal for aquarists seeking a more mature, ready-to-display plant to add instant structure and greenery to their aquascape.

At Aquarium Plants Factory, we cultivate these large-size Java Ferns in emersed conditions, resulting in strong, vibrant leaves and thick rhizomes that transition beautifully when submerged. The classic, sword-like leaves are rich green in color and fan outward in a graceful arch, making them perfect for midground to background placement in medium to large aquariums.

Java Fern is a rhizome-based plant and should always be attached to driftwood, rocks, or other hardscape. Never bury the rhizome in substrate, as this may lead to rot. It is incredibly hardy, low maintenance, and adaptable to a wide range of water parameters—making it suitable for both novice and experienced aquarists.

🌟 Key Features:

  • Special Large Size: Grows approx. 8–10" tall—perfect for instant visual impact

  • Hardy & Adaptable: Tolerates various lighting and water conditions
