products.title: Bucephalandra Red Mini
products.body_html:
🌸 Bucephalandra ‘Red Mini’ – Scarlet Gem of Borneo

Bucephalandra ‘Red Mini’ is a striking and compact Bucephalandra variety renowned for its vivid red-bronze foliage and petite size. Originating from the riverbanks of Borneo, this beautiful plant showcases slender, oval leaves with a deep ruby to copper tone that glows under high or RGB lighting. Its miniature growth habit makes it an ideal choice for nano aquascapes, foreground accents, or driftwood details. Slow-growing, hardy, and easy to maintain, ‘Red Mini’ adds a bold yet refined splash of color to any aquascape.

🌟 Key Features

  • Scientific Name: Bucephalandra sp. ‘Red Mini’

  • Common Name: Buce Red Mini

  • Brilliant Coloration: Deep red to bronze leaves that shimmer with a metallic sheen under aquarium lighting.

  • Miniature Growth Form: Naturally compact and low-profile—perfect for nano tanks and small aquascapes.

  • Leaf Texture: Smooth, rounded leaves that intensify in color under strong or RGB lighting.

  • Hardy & Adaptable: Thrives in a wide range of water parameters and lighting levels.

  • Epiphytic Nature: Attaches easily to driftwood, stones, or hardscape—rhizome must stay above substrate.

🌱 Growth & Propagation

  • Lighting Needs: Low to medium light; stronger lighting enhances red hues and metallic reflections.

  • CO₂ & Fertilizers: Optional, but beneficial for faster growth and richer coloration.

  • Growth Method: Rhizome-based; do not bury the rhizome.

  • Nutrient Substrate: Not required—absorbs nutrients from the water column.

  • Can Be Grown Emersed: Yes – excellent for paludariums and humid terrarium setups.

🖼️ Aquascaping Benefits

  • Infuses vivid red and bronze tones that contrast beautifully with green plants and mosses.

  • Ideal for nano aquascapes, shrimp tanks, or as a bright midground highlight.

  • Perfect for collectors seeking color depth in small, detailed layouts.

  • Adds visual warmth and balance to both low-tech and high-tech aquascapes.

📍 Cultivation & Sustainability

At Aquarium Plants Factory’s nursery, all of our Bucephalandra species are 100% farm-raised—never taken from the wild. Each plant originates ex-vitro (grown from sterile lab culture) before being transitioned to hydroponic growth in our controlled environment. This ensures exceptional quality, pest-free plants, and sustainable preservation of Borneo’s native ecosystems.

📦 What You’ll Receive

Sold as loose Bare-Root or Potted plants:

  • 1 Bare-Root: Approximately 6+ leaves

  • 5 Bare-Root: Approximately 25+ leaves total

  • 10 Bare-Root: Approximately 50+ leaves total

  • 1 Potted: Approximately 6+ leaves

  • 5 Potted: Approximately 25+ leaves total

  • 10 Potted: Approximately 50+ leaves total

⚠️ Disclaimer

Like most Bucephalandra species, minor leaf melt may occur after introduction as the plant adjusts to new water parameters. This is normal and temporary—new submersed leaves will regrow from the rhizome once the plant stabilizes.

Certain aquatic plants may be restricted or regulated in some U.S. states. Always check your local and state regulations before purchasing or introducing plants into natural waterways. Never release aquarium plants into the wild.

products.title: Bucephalandra Alamanda Dark
products.body_html:
Bucephalandra Alamanda Dark is a captivating and rare aquatic plant that brings a touch of sophistication to any aquarium. Known for its deep, rich hues and unique foliage, this plant is a favorite among aquascapers and aquarium enthusiasts. With its easy care requirements and stunning appearance, Bucephalandra Alamanda Dark is perfect for both novice and experienced aquarists.

Key Features of Bucephalandra Alamanda Dark

  1. Deep, Dark Foliage: Bucephalandra Alamanda Dark boasts leaves with a deep, dark green to almost black coloration, creating a striking contrast in your aquarium. The leaves' subtle iridescence adds an extra layer of beauty, making this plant a standout feature in any aquascape.

  2. Compact and Versatile: This plant has a compact growth habit, making it ideal for various aquascaping styles. It can be used as a foreground or midground plant, and its slow growth rate ensures it maintains its shape without frequent trimming.

  3. Easy to Care For: Bucephalandra Alamanda Dark is a hardy plant that thrives in a range of water conditions. It requires low to moderate lighting and can tolerate various water parameters, making it suitable for both low-tech and high-tech setups.

  4. Unique Flowers: In addition to its stunning foliage, Bucephalandra Alamanda Dark occasionally produces small, delicate flowers that add an extra layer of beauty to the aquarium. These flowers typically bloom above the water surface.

  5. Natural Habitat: Native to the fast-flowing streams and rivers of Borneo, this plant grows attached to rocks and submerged wood. Its natural habitat makes it an excellent choice for replicating natural biotope aquariums.

Care Tips for Bucephalandra Alamanda Dark

  • Lighting: Low to moderate lighting is ideal for Bucephalandra Alamanda Dark. Too much light can lead to algae growth on its leaves, while too little light may reduce its vibrant coloration.
  • Water Conditions: This plant prefers water temperatures between 68-78°F (20-26°C) with a pH range of 6.0 to 7.5. It can thrive in both soft and moderately hard water.
  • Substrate: While Bucephalandra Alamanda Dark can be planted in substrate, it often does best when attached to rocks or driftwood, mimicking its natural growing conditions.
  • Fertilization: Regular fertilization with a balanced aquarium fertilizer will promote healthy growth and vibrant colors. CO2 supplementation can enhance growth but is not strictly necessary.
  • Propagation: This plant can be easily propagated by cutting and replanting its rhizomes. Ensure each cutting has a few leaves and roots for successful propagation.

Benefits of Adding Bucephalandra Alamanda Dark to Your Aquarium

  • Aesthetic Appeal: The deep, dark foliage of Bucephalandra Alamanda Dark creates a stunning visual effect, enhancing the overall beauty of your aquascape.
  • Habitat Enrichment: Provides natural hiding spots and surfaces for biofilm, benefiting fish and invertebrates.
  • Water Quality Improvement: Like other aquatic plants, Bucephalandra Alamanda Dark helps maintain water quality by absorbing excess nutrients and oxygenating the water.

Bucephalandra Alamanda Dark is a must-have for any aquarist looking to elevate the beauty and biodiversity of their aquarium. Its unique appearance, combined with its ease of care, makes it a standout choice for creating a vibrant and healthy underwater environment.

For more detailed information and to purchase Bucephalandra Alamanda Dark, visit reputable aquatic plant retailers or specialized aquarium forums.

products.title: Nymphaea Yellow Bangpra (Pin waree) | Yellow Hardy Water Lily
products.body_html:
🌼 Nymphaea ‘Yellow Bangpra’ (Pin Waree) – Hardy Yellow Water Lily

Nymphaea ‘Yellow Bangpra’, also known as ‘Pin Waree’, is a stunning hardy yellow water lily cherished for its sunny golden blooms and graceful floating leaves. Originating from Thailand, this beautiful hybrid combines elegance and durability, producing vibrant flowers that bring warmth and tropical brightness to any pond or water garden. With its consistent blooming habit and easy adaptability, Nymphaea ‘Yellow Bangpra’ adds a radiant, cheerful focal point to your aquatic landscape all season long.

🌟 Key Features:

  • Scientific Name: Nymphaea ‘Yellow Bangpra’ (Synonym: Nymphaea ‘Pin Waree’)

  • Common Name: Yellow Bangpra Water Lily

  • Type: Hardy Water Lily

  • Flower Color: Bright yellow with soft golden tones and a sunny yellow center

  • Flower Form: Star-shaped blooms with multiple layers of narrow, pointed petals

  • Bloom Size: Medium (4–6 inches / 10–15 cm)

  • Foliage: Rounded green leaves with occasional purplish mottling

  • Blooming Season: May through September (long and free-flowering)

  • Fragrance: Light, pleasant scent

  • Growth Spread: Moderate (up to 3 feet / 90 cm)

🌤️ Optimal Growing Conditions:

  • Sunlight: Full sun exposure (6–8 hours daily) for maximum color and bloom production

  • Planting Depth: 12–20 inches (30–50 cm) of water above the rhizome

  • Soil Type: Heavy loam or field soil enriched with 3–5 aquatic fertilizer tablets near the rhizome

  • pH Range: 6.0–8.0 (tolerates both slightly acidic and alkaline water)

  • Temperature Tolerance: Hardy in most climates; can overwinter outdoors when pond does not freeze solid

🖼️ Aquascaping & Pond Benefits:

  • Bright Focal Point: Brilliant yellow blooms add a warm, uplifting tone to any pond design.

  • Healthy Pond Ecosystem: Broad leaves help reduce algae and provide natural shade for fish.

  • Long-Lasting Blooms: Continuous flowering throughout the summer for a vibrant pond display.

  • Low Maintenance: Easy to plant, grow, and propagate, ideal for both beginners and experts.

📦 What You’ll Receive:

One young, viable Nymphaea ‘Yellow Bangpra’ hardy water lily rhizome with a healthy crown and 1–2 actively growing leaves, ensuring strong establishment and reliable flowering after planting.

⚠️ Disclaimer:

All water lilies are shipped bare-root (as tubers) and should be planted immediately upon arrival for best results. Bloom and leaf color, size, and shape may vary slightly depending on lighting, water quality, and plant maturity.

🚫 State Restrictions Notice ♻️ Be a Responsible Aquatic Plants Owner:

Certain aquatic plants may be restricted or regulated in some U.S. states. Always check your local and state regulations before purchasing or introducing plants into natural waterways. Never release aquarium or pond plants into the wild.

PLANTING INSTRUCTIONS FOR WATER LILY

 

There are two main types of water lilies, tropical and hardy. The differences between tropical and hardy water lilies are a growing habit. Tropical water lilies don't do well in winter. Hardy water lilies, on the other hand, can tolerate the cold. Tropical water lilies tubers must be removed from the pond and stored in moist conditions to come back in the future years. In warm climates, they can remain in the pond.

featured_image.alt: Close-up of a healthy hardy water lily rhizome showing new leaf growth, held over a pond background with floating pads.

products.title: Nymphaea Dang Mameaw | Deep Pink Tropical Water Lily
products.body_html:

Renowned for its striking deep pink to raspberry-red flowers, which measure 4” to 8” across, this variety is both a visual and olfactory delight. Complemented by lush green leaves, approximately 6” to 12” in size, and a spread of 3’ to 7’, 'Dang Mameaw' creates a captivating and fragrant display.

Optimal Conditions:

  • Sunlight Requirements: As a day-blooming variety, Nymphaea 'Dang Mameaw' thrives in full sunlight, which enhances the color and fragrance of its flowers.

Benefits:

Care and Maintenance:

  • Robust Growth: With proper care and optimal water depth, this water lily will thrive and provide a consistent display of beautiful and fragrant flowers.

Conclusion:

Nymphaea 'Dang Mameaw' (Dang Ma Miew Water Lily) is a must-have for any water garden enthusiast looking to add a splash of color and fragrance to their pond. Its deep pink to raspberry-red blooms, lush green foliage, and delightful scent make it a standout choice. Plant Nymphaea 'Dang Mameaw' in your pond to enjoy the vibrant beauty and fragrant atmosphere it brings.

products.title: Myriophyllum Guyana Vitro Culture
products.body_html:
Myriophyllum Guyana is a beautiful and versatile aquatic plant that adds elegance to any aquarium. Known for its delicate, feathery foliage, this plant is a favorite among aquascaping enthusiasts looking to create lush, green underwater landscapes.

Key Features

  • Scientific Name: Myriophyllum Guyana
  • Common Name: Myriophyllum Guyana
  • Family: Haloragaceae
  • Leaf Color: Bright green
  • Growth Form: Feathery, bushy

Unique Characteristics

Myriophyllum Guyana is renowned for its finely divided, bright green leaves that create a soft, feathery appearance. This plant’s graceful and bushy growth makes it an excellent choice for adding texture and depth to your aquarium, creating a natural and serene environment.

Ideal Conditions

To ensure optimal growth for Myriophyllum Guyana, provide the following conditions:

  • Light: Moderate to high intensity
  • CO2: Beneficial but not essential
  • Substrate: Nutrient-rich substrate preferred
  • Water Temperature: 20-28°C (68-82°F)
  • pH Range: 6.0-7.5

Care and Maintenance

Myriophyllum Guyana is relatively easy to care for, making it suitable for both beginners and experienced aquarists:

  • Lighting: Ensure moderate to high light levels to promote healthy growth.
  • CO2 Injection: While not required, CO2 supplementation can enhance growth and overall health.
  • Trimming: Regular trimming helps maintain its shape and encourages bushier growth.
  • Fertilization: Occasional fertilization supports vibrant growth and health.

Aquascaping Uses

Myriophyllum Guyana is highly versatile and can be used in various ways:

  • Midground Plant: Ideal for adding density and vibrant green color to the midground area.
  • Background Plant: Can be used to create a lush green backdrop.
  • Accent Plant: Its feathery leaves make it an excellent focal point in aquascapes.

The bright green, feathery foliage of Myriophyllum Guyana provides excellent contrast and visual interest, making it a great choice for both Dutch and Nature-style aquascapes.

Propagation

Propagation of Myriophyllum Guyana is simple and effective. This plant propagates through stem cuttings. Simply cut a healthy stem and replant it in the substrate, where it will develop roots and grow into a new plant.

Myriophyllum Guyana is a must-have for any aquarist looking to enhance their aquarium with a graceful and eye-catching plant. Its delicate, feathery leaves and bushy growth make it a standout choice for creating a beautiful, natural underwater landscape.

products.title: Bolbitis sp. Buea
products.body_html:
🌿 Bolbitis sp. 'Buea' – Rare African Water Fern with Elegant, Textured Fronds

Bolbitis sp. 'Buea', commonly known as the Buea Fern, is a rare and highly sought-after aquatic fern from the river systems near Buea, Cameroon, in Central Africa. Known for its gracefully arching, deep green fronds with a lightly textured surface, this plant brings a refined and natural look to any aquascape. Like other Bolbitis species, it thrives best when attached to hardscape rather than planted in substrate, making it an excellent choice for driftwood- and stone-centered layouts.

Unlike the more common Bolbitis heudelotii, the Buea Fern has slightly narrower fronds with a delicate, almost lace-like quality, adding a unique dimension to planted tanks.

🌟 Key Features:

  • Scientific Name: Bolbitis sp. 'Buea'

  • Synonyms: Didymoglossum erosum

  • Common Name: Buea Fern

  • Origin: Cameroon, Central Africa

  • Growth Form: Rhizome-based aquatic fern with arching, textured fronds

  • Placement: Best suited for midground or background, attached to wood or rock

  • Lighting: Low to moderate light, avoiding direct high-intensity light to prevent algae growth

  • CO₂ & Nutrients: Appreciates CO₂ and nutrient-rich water for optimal growth

🌱 Growth & Propagation:

  • Lighting Needs: Low to medium (avoid strong, direct light)

  • CO₂ & Fertilizers: CO₂ recommended; benefits from regular dosing of liquid fertilizers

  • Growth Method: Rhizome attachment to hardscape (never bury rhizome in substrate)

  • Nutrient Substrate: Not required

  • Can Be Grown Emersed: Yes, in high-humidity terrariums or paludariums

🖼️ Aquascaping Benefits:

  • Adds lush, natural greenery to shaded aquarium areas

  • Provides shelter for shrimp and small fish

  • Slow-growing and algae-resistant under proper conditions

📦 What You’ll Receive:

Sold as a small portion

  • Small portion: One loose portion, approx. 2" x 2" in size

  • This moss is dark green in color

⚠️ Disclaimer:
Bolbitis species are sensitive to unstable water parameters and can be slow to adapt to new environments. Growth may temporarily stall during acclimation. Avoid burying the rhizome in substrate, as this can cause rot.

🚫 State Restrictions Notice ♻️ Be a Responsible Aquatic Plants Owner
Never release aquarium plants into natural waterways. Always dispose of unwanted plants responsibly.

products.title: Nymphaea Murasaki Shikibu | White & Violet Tropical Water Lily
products.body_html:
🌸 Nymphaea Murasaki Shikibu – Rare Tropical Day-Blooming Waterlily

Nymphaea ‘Murasaki Shikibu’ is a breathtaking and exceptionally rare tropical day-blooming waterlily prized for its radiant violet blooms and graceful floating leaves. Named after the famous Japanese author of The Tale of Genji, this variety embodies elegance and refinement in any pond or water garden.

🌟 Key Features

  • Scientific Name: Nymphaea ‘Murasaki Shikibu’

  • Type: Rare tropical day-blooming waterlily

  • Bloom Color: Striking shades of deep violet to purple with delicate petal layering

  • Leaves: Rounded floating pads, often lightly mottled for added contrast

  • Blooming Habit: Opens in the morning and closes by late afternoon under sunlight

  • Fragrance: Mild, sweet floral scent

🌱 Optimal Growing Conditions

  • Lighting Needs: Full sun (6+ hours daily) encourages maximum flowering

  • Water Depth: Ideal planting depth of 12–24 inches (measured from soil surface to water surface)

  • Temperature Range: Thrives in warm water 75–85°F (24–29°C)

  • Fertilization: Use aquatic plant fertilizer tablets monthly during growing season for continuous blooms

  • Hardiness: Best suited for USDA Zones 9–11; must be overwintered indoors in cooler climates

🖼️ Aquascaping & Pond Benefits

  • Centerpiece Beauty: Vibrant purple blooms provide a dramatic focal point in ponds and water features

  • Shade & Shelter: Floating pads help reduce algae growth and provide refuge for koi and goldfish

  • Collector’s Gem: Highly sought after by waterlily enthusiasts for its rarity and cultural significance

    There are two main types of water lilies, tropical and hardy. The differences between tropical and hardy water lilies are a growing habit. Tropical water lilies don't do well in winter. In warm climates, they can remain in the pond.

When you receive your Tropical Water Lilies Packages [Grower's Choice], you should follow the instructions that come with them for planting and care. However, if you live in a colder region, it is important to note that tropical water lilies may not survive the winter outdoors, and you may need to bring them indoors or replace them with hardy water lilies.

There are two main types of water lilies, tropical and hardy. The differences between tropical and hardy water lilies are a growing habit. Tropical water lilies don't do well in winter. Hardy water lilies, on the other hand, can tolerate the cold. Tropical water lilies tubers must be removed from the pond and stored in moist conditions to come back in the future years. In warm climates, they can remain in the pond.

products.title: Samolus Parviflorus Red
products.body_html:
Samolus parviflorus 'Red' — A Rare Splash of Red for Your Aquascape

Samolus parviflorus 'Red' is a unique and visually striking aquatic plant prized for its vivid coloration and easy adaptability. Native to the limestone-rich rivers of South China, this rare beauty offers a rosette-style growth pattern and deep red hues when grown submerged—making it an exceptional choice for aquascapers who want to introduce bold, natural tones into their underwater landscapes.

Unlike many "red" plants that require high maintenance, Samolus 'Red' thrives with moderate care, making it accessible for both beginners and experienced hobbyists.

Key Features, Benefits & Growing Tips:

🔴 Stunning Red Coloration:
Emerges green when grown emersed but transitions into brilliant shades of red when fully submerged under moderate to high light.

🌿 Rosette Growth Form:
Creates low, compact clusters perfect for midground accents or foreground highlights in planted aquariums.

💧 Adaptable & Easy to Care For:
Tolerates a wide range of water parameters and grows well with or without CO₂ injection (though CO₂ and strong lighting intensify the red coloration).

✂️ Simple Propagation:
Can be propagated easily by replanting side shoots or separated stems, allowing for fast carpet expansion.

🐠 Perfect For:
Nature aquariums, contrast-driven layouts, biotope-style tanks, and hobbyists wanting a rare, standout species in their collection.

Why Choose Samolus parviflorus 'Red' for Your Planted Aquarium?

Adding depth and vibrant contrast to a planted tank can be challenging—but Samolus parviflorus 'Red' makes it effortless. Its brilliant submerged coloration immediately draws the eye, providing a bold counterbalance to green foliage plants. Hardy, undemanding, and visually captivating, it’s an excellent choice for aquascapers aiming to create a dynamic and colorful aquascape without the intense care often associated with red plants.

Whether you’re crafting a lush nature aquarium, designing a contest-level tank, or simply want something rare and beautiful, Samolus 'Red' is a rewarding and impactful addition.

products.title: Java Fern Sunrise
products.body_html:
🌿 Microsorum pteropus ‘Sunrise’ – Bronze-Tinted Java Fern with Unique Leaf Form

Microsorum pteropus ‘Sunrise’, also known as Java Fern Sunrise, is a rare and eye-catching variety of the classic Java Fern. This cultivar features slender, elongated leaves with subtle three-pointed tips, displaying a beautiful range of coloration from rich green to bronze under the right lighting. Occasionally, new growth may take on a reddish tint, giving your aquascape a soft touch of warmth and contrast.

At Aquarium Plants Factory, we grow this plant emersed in our nursery, where it develops healthy structure and vibrant form. Once submerged in your aquarium, it transitions beautifully and thrives with minimal care. Like all Java Ferns, Sunrise is a rhizome-based plant and should be attached to driftwood, stone, or hardscape—never buried in substrate.

Its slow growth and graceful shape make it ideal for aquascapers looking to add texture and gentle movement to midground or background areas in both low- and high-tech setups.

🌟 Key Features:

  • Subtle Red-Bronze Highlights: New leaves may develop reddish or bronze tones under strong light

  • Elegant Growth Form: Slender, narrow leaves with occasional three-pointed tips

  • Attachable Rhizome Plant: Grows best on rocks, driftwood, or decor—no planting required

  • Low Maintenance: Thrives without CO₂ and under low to high lighting

  • Beginner Friendly: Easy to care for and slow-growing, great for low-tech tanks

🔎 Botanical Information:

  • Scientific Name: Microsorum pteropus ‘Sunrise’

  • Synonyms: Leptochilus pteropus ‘Sunrise’

  • Common Names: Java Fern Sunrise

  • Plant Type: Rhizome plant

  • Placement: Attach to driftwood, stone, or hardscape

  • Growth Rate: Slow

  • Lighting: Low to High

💡 Optimal Growing Conditions:

  • Lighting: Performs well under low to high light—reddish tones emerge with brighter light

  • CO₂ & Fertilization: Not required; occasional liquid fertilizer boosts health and color

  • Water Parameters:

🌱 Growth & Propagation:

products.title: Lobelia Cardinalis Wavy
products.body_html:
🌿 Lobelia Cardinalis Wavy – Compact, Wavy-Leaf Aquatic Plant

Lobelia Cardinalis Wavy is a slow-growing, compact aquarium plant distinguished by its beautifully curled, ruffled leaf edges. This hardy selection of the Cardinal Flower has a very bushy underwater growth form, making it ideal for smaller aquariums and precise aquascaping layouts. While its leaf size is similar to the original Lobelia cardinalis, its denser, more compact habit offers a unique texture and structure for your tank.

At Aquarium Plants Factory nursery, we cultivate Lobelia Cardinalis Wavy in its emersed form, where the tops of the leaves display a fresh green hue while the undersides show a striking red-purple color. When grown submerged, the foliage transitions to lush green, with its distinctive wavy edges adding visual interest. This variety is still relatively new and rarely available in the aquarium trade, making it a sought-after choice for collectors and aquascaping enthusiasts.

products.title: Anubias Petite White Platinum Vitro Culture
products.body_html:
Anubias Petite White Platinum is a rare and exquisite variety of the popular Anubias aquarium plant, renowned for its stunning albino white leaves. Native to Africa, this compact plant adds an elegant touch to any aquascape with its unique coloration and manageable size. Although it is hard to grow and has a very slow growth rate, its unique beauty makes it highly sought after by dedicated aquarists.

Key Features:

  • Albino White Leaves: Features striking white leaves that add a touch of elegance and uniqueness to your aquarium.
  • Compact Size: A petite version of Anubias, perfect for creating intricate and detailed aquascapes.
  • Slow Growth: Grows very slowly and requires careful attention, making it a rewarding challenge for experienced aquarists.

Optimal Conditions:

  • Lighting: Thrives in low to moderate lighting conditions, though it requires stable light levels to maintain its health.
  • Water Parameters: Prefers stable water conditions and is best suited for aquariums maintained by experienced hobbyists.

Planting and Care:

  • Attachment: Can be attached to rocks, driftwood, or other decorations using fishing line or thread.
  • Rhizome Placement: Ensure the rhizome (the thick stem from which leaves emerge) is not buried in the substrate to prevent rot. Attach it to a solid surface or plant it with the rhizome above the substrate.
  • Fertilization and Pruning: Requires minimal fertilizer and occasional pruning to maintain its size and shape. Regular monitoring and stable conditions are key to its survival.

Benefits:

  • Unique Aesthetic: Its rare albino white leaves enhance the visual appeal of any aquarium, providing a striking contrast to green foliage.
  • Hardiness: Despite its slow growth, Anubias White Platinum Petite is known for its durability and can thrive with careful maintenance.
  • Rewarding Challenge: Ideal for experienced aquarists looking for a unique and sophisticated addition to their tank.

Market Rarity:

  • Rare and Elegant: Extremely rare in the market, often priced higher than regular Anubias species due to its scarcity and unique appearance.

Conclusion:

Anubias Petite White Platinum is a stunning and low-maintenance plant that adds a unique and elegant touch to any aquarium. While it is hard to grow and grows very slowly, its rare albino white leaves make it a standout choice for aquarists seeking a rare and sophisticated plant. With careful attention and stable conditions, this plant can become a highlight of your aquatic environment.

Enhance your aquarium with the exquisite beauty of Anubias Petite White Platinum and enjoy the unique transformation it brings to your aquatic world.

Ludwigia palustris 'Green' – Vibrant Green Stem Plant for Lush Aquascaping

Ludwigia palustris 'Green' is a vibrant stem plant known for its lush, bright green foliage and adaptability to a wide range of aquarium conditions. Unlike its red counterparts, this green variety offers a crisp, clean aesthetic that adds refreshing contrast and fullness to any aquascape. Its fast growth and versatility make it ideal for midground placement or as a bushy background accent when grouped in larger tanks.

Whether you're creating a nature-style layout or a Dutch-style display, Ludwigia palustris 'Green' provides reliable structure, color, and ease of maintenance.

🌿 Key Features:

  • Scientific Name: Ludwigia palustris 'Green'

  • Common Name: Green Ludwigia, Ludwigia Green

  • Leaf Color: Bright, vibrant green under submerged conditions

  • Growth Habit: Upright, fast-growing stem plant

  • Lighting Requirement: Medium to high for compact, lush growth

  • CO₂ Requirement: Optional; improves density and coloration

  • Temperature Range: 64–82°F (18–28°C)

  • Water Parameters: pH 5.5–7.5; soft to moderately hard water

  • Growth Speed: Fast

🧪 Optimal Growing Conditions:

  • Lighting: Medium to high for best leaf structure and compact form

  • Temperature: 72–78°F optimal; adaptable across a wide range

  • CO₂: Not required, but enhances growth and appearance

  • Fertilization: Benefits from water column dosing and micronutrients

  • Trimming: Responds well to frequent trimming for bushy appearance

💡 Aquascaping Benefits:

  • Fresh Color Palette: Adds light green contrast to red or dark-toned plants

  • Midground to Background Use: Perfect as a mid-height filler or background grouping

  • Easy to Shape: Great for dense clusters, rows, or trimming into defined hedges

  • Adaptable: Suitable for both beginner and experienced aquascapers

  • Versatile Style: Complements nature, Dutch, and jungle-style aquascapes

🌱 Propagation & Growth:

  • Growth Speed: Fast-growing with regular maintenance

  • Propagation Method: Stem cuttings—replant trimmed tops directly into substrate

  • Nutrient Substrate: Not Required

  • Can Be Grown Emersed: Yes – excellent for paludariums and wet soil setups

📦 What You’ll Receive:

Enhance your aquarium with our fresh, high-quality plants, available in convenient formats:

  • Fresh-Cut Plants: Approx. 6 loose, fresh-cut stems without roots

  • Bunched Plants: Approx. 6 fresh-cut stems bundled with foam and lead weight

  • Potted Plants: Approx. 6 fresh-cut stems potted in rockwool with a net pot

⚠️ State Restrictions Notice ♻️ Be a Responsible Aquatic Plants Owner
Please never release Ludwigia or any aquatic plant into local waterways. Dispose of trimmings responsibly. This species is not currently restricted in the U.S., but local regulations may apply.

products.title: Butterfly Moss
products.body_html:
Butterfly Moss – Rare Aquatic Moss with Wing-Like Leaves

Butterfly Moss (Plagiomnium sp. 'Butterfly') is a rare and captivating liverwort that brings natural beauty and structure to freshwater aquariums. Renowned for its rounded, overlapping leaves that resemble butterfly wings, this moss adds gentle texture and lush greenery to both nature-style and minimalist aquascapes. Despite its delicate appearance, Butterfly Moss is hardy, easy to care for, and suitable for a wide range of tank setups.

Whether you're designing a serene shrimp habitat or a detailed moss wall, Plagiomnium sp. 'Butterfly' is a superb choice for aquarists seeking rare, low-maintenance plants that offer both aesthetics and habitat benefits.

🌿 Key Features of Butterfly Moss

  • Scientific Name: Plagiomnium sp. 'Butterfly'

  • Common Name: Butterfly Moss

  • Leaf Structure: Small, rounded leaves resembling butterfly wings

  • Rhizoids Present: Hairlike structures often mistaken for algae serve as anchoring roots

  • Versatile Use: Attachable to driftwood, stone, or substrate for foreground, midground, or vertical layouts

  • Moderate Growth Rate: Forms lush green mats over time, requiring minimal trimming

✅ Optimal Growing Conditions

  • Lighting: Low to medium (avoid direct, intense light to prevent drying)

  • CO₂: Not required, though CO₂ supplementation can promote fuller growth

  • Substrate: Not required—best attached to hardscape elements

  • Water Parameters:

    • pH: 6.0–7.5

    • Temperature: 68–77°F (20–25°C)

🐠 Aquascaping Benefits

  • Aesthetic Focal Point: Unique, wing-like foliage provides visual interest in nature aquariums

  • Shelter for Aquatic Life: Offers protective hiding places for shrimp, fry, and small fish

  • Beginner Friendly: Low care requirements and slow, manageable growth

  • Custom Layout Potential: Can be attached to mesh, rocks, driftwood, or left free-floating

🌱 Why Choose Butterfly Moss?

Butterfly Moss is an ideal choice for aquascapers who appreciate delicate structure with strong visual impact. Its elegant leaf pattern, ability to thrive in low-tech setups, and benefits to tank biodiversity make it a favorite for both seasoned and novice hobbyists. Whether used as a moss carpet or a cascading accent, it brings life and balance to any aquascape.

📦 What You’ll Receive

  • Loose Portion: A small loose portion of Butterfly Moss, approximately 2" x 2" in size

  • Ready to Attach: Ideal for gluing or tying to hardscape; will self-adhere over time

  • Note: Occasionally may include a mix of other moss species due to its delicate nature

🔒 Disclaimer

Pictures are sample representations. Actual plants may vary slightly in size, shape, and density. Unless specifically noted, plants are not guaranteed to be pest- or pesticide-free, or grown in a particular form (emersed or submerged).

⚠️ State Restrictions Notice ♻️ Be a Responsible Aquatic Plants Owner

Please never release Butterfly Moss—or any aquatic plant—into local waterways. Improper disposal can harm native ecosystems. Always discard plants responsibly.

products.title: Hygrophila Siamensis 53B
products.body_html:
Hygrophila corymbosa 'Siamensis 53B' is a variety of Hygrophila corymbosa 'Siamensis'. This Hygrophila 'Siamensis 53B' forms an incredible amount of side shoots and becomes bushy relatively rapidly.
Stems become 15-40 cm tall and 7-12 cm wide. Its beautiful bright green leaves are narrower than the regular 'Siamensis' and it can achieve red-brown leaves in good light conditions. An undemanding, fast-growing plant that requires frequent pruning to maintain bushy growth. Replant cut-off shoots, they will soon form new roots and grow.

products.title: Echinodorus Pink Miracle
products.body_html:
Echinodorus 'Pink Miracle' – Stunning Hybrid Sword Plant with Soft Pink Elegance

Echinodorus 'Pink Miracle' is a remarkable hybrid between Echinodorus horemanii 'Red' and Echinodorus barthii, selectively bred to showcase vibrant pink to reddish hues in a graceful, upright rosette form. This rare sword plant variety stands out for its soft, pastel pink tones and refined leaf structure, making it an exquisite centerpiece in medium to large aquariums.

As a rooted substrate plant, it thrives best in nutrient-rich conditions and benefits greatly from strong lighting and CO₂ supplementation. Whether you're creating a bold focal point or complementing a vibrant aquascape layout, Echinodorus 'Pink Miracle' brings elegance and color to your underwater world.

🌿 Key Features:

  • Scientific Name: Echinodorus 'Pink Miracle'

  • Parentage: Echinodorus horemanii 'Red' × Echinodorus barthii

  • Leaf Color: Pink to reddish-pink with light veining and soft green undertones

  • Growth Habit: Upright, rosette-forming sword plant

  • Lighting Requirement: Medium to high for best coloration

  • CO₂ Requirement: Recommended but not required

  • Temperature Range: 68–82°F (20–28°C)

  • Water Parameters: pH 6.0–7.5; soft to moderately hard water

  • Growth Speed: Moderate

🧪 Optimal Growing Conditions:

  • Lighting: Medium to high intensity for vibrant pink tones

  • Temperature: Prefers stable temps between 72–78°F

  • Substrate: Requires nutrient-rich substrate for optimal root development

  • CO₂: Optional but enhances color, size, and health

  • Fertilization: Root tabs and liquid fertilizers highly beneficial

💡 Aquascaping Benefits:

  • Color Accent: Unique pink tones provide striking contrast to green background plants

  • Midground to Background Use: Ideal as a feature plant or focal rosette in open aquascapes

  • Hybrid Strength: Combines the elegant form of horemanii with the robust growth of barthii

  • Suitable for Larger Tanks: Best used in 10-gallon and up due to its eventual size

  • Low Maintenance: Once established, requires minimal trimming

🌱 Propagation & Growth:

  • Growth Speed: Moderate

  • Propagation Method: Produces side shoots or runners with plantlets over time

  • Nutrient Substrate: Required

  • Can Be Grown Emersed: Yes – suitable for emersed tanks and humid terrarium-style setups

📦 What You’ll Receive:

Enhance your aquarium with our fresh, high-quality plants, available in convenient formats:

  • Bare-Root Plants: One single, loose small plant

  • Rooted Bunched: Approx. 1–2 bare-root plants bundled with foam and plant weight for stability

  • Potted Plants: Approx. 1–2 rooted plants securely potted in rockwool within a net pot

⚠️ State Restrictions Notice ♻️ Be a Responsible Aquatic Plants Owner
Never release Echinodorus or any aquatic plant into natural waterways. Dispose of trimmings responsibly. This species is not restricted in the U.S., but local regulations may apply.

products.title: Ludwigia Ovalis Pink
products.body_html:
Ludwigia ovalis, a standout in the aquarium hobby, is celebrated for its vivid green leaves and ease of cultivation. Native to the southeastern United States, this Onagraceae family member is a fast-growing, flowering aquatic plant, perfect for adding depth and color to aquariums. Its oval-shaped leaves, which can turn a stunning reddish-pink under high lighting, grow along stems reaching 20-30 cm. Ideal for both background and midground placement, Ludwigia ovalis enhances aquascapes with its height and vibrant hues, while providing shelter for aquatic life.

This plant thrives under moderate to high light and benefits from CO2 and nutrient-rich substrates, making it a versatile choice for varying aquarium conditions. Regular pruning is recommended to maintain its shape and prevent overgrowth. Ludwigia ovalis is a preferred choice among aquascapers and hobbyists for its striking contrast and easy maintenance. Be aware of mislabeling in the market, especially confusion with Ludwigia Atlantis; thorough research is advised to ensure authentic Ludwigia ovalis.
